({ asm volatile("1626" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1626b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1626)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" "" : : [fmt] "i"
(""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line] "i" (2349), [
fl] "i" (0), [size] "i" (sizeof(struct bug_entry))); } while (
0); __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0); } while (0);
2350
2351 ac->ac_found++;
2352 ac->ac_cX_found[ac->ac_criteria]++;
2353
2354 /*
2355 * The special case - take what you catch first
2356 */
2357 if (unlikely(ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_FIRST)__builtin_expect(!!(ac->ac_flags & 0x0008), 0)) {
2358 *bex = *ex;
2359 ext4_mb_use_best_found(ac, e4b);
2360 return;
2361 }
2362
2363 /*
2364 * Let's check whether the chuck is good enough
2365 */
2366 if (ex->fe_len == gex->fe_len) {
2367 *bex = *ex;
2368 ext4_mb_use_best_found(ac, e4b);
2369 return;
2370 }
2371
2372 /*
2373 * If this is first found extent, just store it in the context
2374 */
2375 if (bex->fe_len == 0) {
2376 *bex = *ex;
2377 return;
2378 }
2379
2380 /*
2381 * If new found extent is better, store it in the context
2382 */
2383 if (bex->fe_len < gex->fe_len) {
2384 /* if the request isn't satisfied, any found extent
2385 * larger than previous best one is better */
2386 if (ex->fe_len > bex->fe_len)
2387 *bex = *ex;
2388 } else if (ex->fe_len > gex->fe_len) {
2389 /* if the request is satisfied, then we try to find
2390 * an extent that still satisfy the request, but is
2391 * smaller than previous one */
2392 if (ex->fe_len < bex->fe_len)
2393 *bex = *ex;
2394 }
2395
2396 ext4_mb_check_limits(ac, e4b, 0);
2397}
2398
2399static noinline_for_stack__attribute__((__noinline__))
2400void ext4_mb_try_best_found(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
2401 struct ext4_buddy *e4b)
2402{
2403 struct ext4_free_extent ex = ac->ac_b_ex;
2404 ext4_group_t group = ex.fe_group;
2405 int max;
2406 int err;
2407
2408 BUG_ON(ex.fe_len <= 0)do { if (__builtin_expect(!!(ex.fe_len <= 0), 0)) do { ({ asm
volatile("1627" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1627b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1627)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"

2426 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(e4b);

2428
2429static noinline_for_stack__attribute__((__noinline__))
2430int ext4_mb_find_by_goal(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
2431 struct ext4_buddy *e4b)
2432{
2433 ext4_group_t group = ac->ac_g_ex.fe_group;
2434 int max;
2435 int err;
2436 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(ac->ac_sb);
2437 struct ext4_group_info *grp = ext4_get_group_info(ac->ac_sb, group);
2438 struct ext4_free_extent ex;
2439
2440 if (!grp)
2441 return -EFSCORRUPTED117;
2442 if (!(ac->ac_flags & (EXT4_MB_HINT_TRY_GOAL0x0200 | EXT4_MB_HINT_GOAL_ONLY0x0100)))
2443 return 0;
2444 if (grp->bb_free == 0)
2445 return 0;
2446
2447 err = ext4_mb_load_buddy(ac->ac_sb, group, e4b);
2448 if (err) {
2449 if (EXT4_MB_GRP_BBITMAP_CORRUPT(e4b->bd_info)(((__builtin_constant_p(2) && __builtin_constant_p((uintptr_t
)(&((e4b->bd_info)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void
*)0)) && (uintptr_t)(&((e4b->bd_info)->bb_state
)) != (uintptr_t)((void *)0) && __builtin_constant_p(
*(const unsigned long *)(&((e4b->bd_info)->bb_state
)))) ? const_test_bit(2, &((e4b->bd_info)->bb_state
)) : _test_bit(2, &((e4b->bd_info)->bb_state)))) &&
2450 !(ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_GOAL_ONLY0x0100))
2451 return 0;
2452 return err;
2453 }
2454
2455 ext4_lock_group(ac->ac_sb, group);
2456 if (unlikely(EXT4_MB_GRP_BBITMAP_CORRUPT(e4b->bd_info))__builtin_expect(!!((((__builtin_constant_p(2) && __builtin_constant_p
((uintptr_t)(&((e4b->bd_info)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t
)((void *)0)) && (uintptr_t)(&((e4b->bd_info)->
bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void *)0) && __builtin_constant_p
(*(const unsigned long *)(&((e4b->bd_info)->bb_state
)))) ? const_test_bit(2, &((e4b->bd_info)->bb_state
)) : _test_bit(2, &((e4b->bd_info)->bb_state))))), 0
))
2457 goto out;
2458
2459 max = mb_find_extent(e4b, ac->ac_g_ex.fe_start,
2460 ac->ac_g_ex.fe_len, &ex);
2461 ex.fe_logical = 0xDEADFA11; /* debug value */
2462
2463 if (max >= ac->ac_g_ex.fe_len &&
2464 ac->ac_g_ex.fe_len == EXT4_NUM_B2C(sbi, sbi->s_stripe)(((sbi->s_stripe) + (sbi)->s_cluster_ratio - 1) >>
(sbi)->s_cluster_bits)) {
2465 ext4_fsblk_t start;
2466
2467 start = ext4_grp_offs_to_block(ac->ac_sb, &ex);
2468 /* use do_div to get remainder (would be 64-bit modulo) */
2469 if (do_div(start, sbi->s_stripe)({ uint32_t __base = (sbi->s_stripe); uint32_t __rem; __rem
= ((uint64_t)(start)) % __base; (start) = ((uint64_t)(start)
) / __base; __rem; }) == 0) {
2470 ac->ac_found++;
2471 ac->ac_b_ex = ex;

2498/*

2550
2551/*
2552 * The routine scans the group and measures all found extents.
2553 * In order to optimize scanning, caller must pass number of
2554 * free blocks in the group, so the routine can know upper limit.
2555 */
2556static noinline_for_stack__attribute__((__noinline__))
2557void ext4_mb_complex_scan_group(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
2558 struct ext4_buddy *e4b)
2559{
2560 struct super_block *sb = ac->ac_sb;
2561 void *bitmap = e4b->bd_bitmap;
2562 struct ext4_free_extent ex;
2563 int i, j, freelen;
2564 int free;
2565
2566 free = e4b->bd_info->bb_free;
2567 if (WARN_ON(free <= 0)({ int __ret_warn_on = !!(free <= 0); if (__builtin_expect

2634
2635/*
2636 * This is a special case for storages like raid5
2637 * we try to find stripe-aligned chunks for stripe-size-multiple requests
2638 */
2639static noinline_for_stack__attribute__((__noinline__))
2640void ext4_mb_scan_aligned(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
2641 struct ext4_buddy *e4b)
2642{
2643 struct super_block *sb = ac->ac_sb;
2644 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(sb);
2645 void *bitmap = e4b->bd_bitmap;
2646 struct ext4_free_extent ex;
2647 ext4_fsblk_t first_group_block;
2648 ext4_fsblk_t a;
2649 ext4_grpblk_t i, stripe;
2650 int max;
2651
2652 BUG_ON(sbi->s_stripe == 0)do { if (__builtin_expect(!!(sbi->s_stripe == 0), 0)) do {
({ asm volatile("1641" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1641b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1641)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" "" : : [fmt] "i"
(""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line] "i" (2652), [
fl] "i" (0), [size] "i" (sizeof(struct bug_entry))); } while (
0); __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0); } while (0);
2653
2654 /* find first stripe-aligned block in group */
2655 first_group_block = ext4_group_first_block_no(sb, e4b->bd_group);
2656
2657 a = first_group_block + sbi->s_stripe - 1;
2658 do_div(a, sbi->s_stripe)({ uint32_t __base = (sbi->s_stripe); uint32_t __rem; __rem
= ((uint64_t)(a)) % __base; (a) = ((uint64_t)(a)) / __base; __rem
; });
2659 i = (a * sbi->s_stripe) - first_group_block;
2660
2661 stripe = EXT4_NUM_B2C(sbi, sbi->s_stripe)(((sbi->s_stripe) + (sbi)->s_cluster_ratio - 1) >>
(sbi)->s_cluster_bits);
2662 i = EXT4_B2C(sbi, i)((i) >> (sbi)->s_cluster_bits);
2663 while (i < EXT4_CLUSTERS_PER_GROUP(sb)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_clusters_per_group)) {
2664 if (!mb_test_bit(i, bitmap)) {
2665 max = mb_find_extent(e4b, i, stripe, &ex);
2666 if (max >= stripe) {
2667 ac->ac_found++;
2668 ac->ac_cX_found[ac->ac_criteria]++;
2669 ex.fe_logical = 0xDEADF00D; /* debug value */
2670 ac->ac_b_ex = ex;
2671 ext4_mb_use_best_found(ac, e4b);
2672 break;
2673 }
2674 }
2675 i += stripe;
2676 }
2677}
2678
2679static void __ext4_mb_scan_group(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac)
2680{
2681 bool is_stripe_aligned;
2682 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi;
2683 enum criteria cr = ac->ac_criteria;
2684
2685 ac->ac_groups_scanned++;
2686 if (cr == CR_POWER2_ALIGNED)
2687 return ext4_mb_simple_scan_group(ac, ac->ac_e4b);
2688
2689 sbi = EXT4_SB(ac->ac_sb);
2690 is_stripe_aligned = false;
2691 if ((sbi->s_stripe >= sbi->s_cluster_ratio) &&
2692 !(ac->ac_g_ex.fe_len % EXT4_NUM_B2C(sbi, sbi->s_stripe)(((sbi->s_stripe) + (sbi)->s_cluster_ratio - 1) >>
(sbi)->s_cluster_bits)))
2693 is_stripe_aligned = true;
2694
2695 if ((cr == CR_GOAL_LEN_FAST || cr == CR_BEST_AVAIL_LEN) &&
2696 is_stripe_aligned)
2697 ext4_mb_scan_aligned(ac, ac->ac_e4b);
2698
2699 if (ac->ac_status == AC_STATUS_CONTINUE1)
2700 ext4_mb_complex_scan_group(ac, ac->ac_e4b);
2701}
2702
2703/*
2704 * This is also called BEFORE we load the buddy bitmap.
2705 * Returns either 1 or 0 indicating that the group is either suitable

2707 */

__builtin_constant_p((uintptr_t)(&((grp)->bb_state)) !=

: : "i" (1643)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "

2765
2766/*
2767 * This could return negative error code if something goes wrong
2768 * during ext4_mb_init_group(). This should not be called with
2769 * ext4_lock_group() held.
2770 *
2771 * Note: because we are conditionally operating with the group lock in
2772 * the EXT4_MB_STRICT_CHECK case, we need to fake out sparse in this
2773 * function using __acquire and __release. This means we need to be
2774 * super careful before messing with the error path handling via "goto
2775 * out"!
2776 */
2777static int ext4_mb_good_group_nolock(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
2778 ext4_group_t group, enum criteria cr)
2779{
2780 struct ext4_group_info *grp = ext4_get_group_info(ac->ac_sb, group);
2781 struct super_block *sb = ac->ac_sb;
2782 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(sb);
2783 bool should_lock = ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_STRICT_CHECK0x4000;
2784 ext4_grpblk_t free;
2785 int ret = 0;
2786
2787 if (!grp)
2788 return -EFSCORRUPTED117;
2789 if (sbi->s_mb_stats)
2790 atomic64_inc(&sbi->s_bal_cX_groups_considered[ac->ac_criteria]);
2791 if (should_lock) {
2792 ext4_lock_group(sb, group);
2793 __release(ext4_group_lock_ptr(sb, group))do { } while (0);
2794 }
2795 free = grp->bb_free;
2796 if (free == 0)
2797 goto out;
2798 /*
2799 * In all criterias except CR_ANY_FREE we try to avoid groups that
2800 * can't possibly satisfy the full goal request due to insufficient
2801 * free blocks.
2802 */
2803 if (cr < CR_ANY_FREE && free < ac->ac_g_ex.fe_len)
2804 goto out;
2805 if (unlikely(EXT4_MB_GRP_BBITMAP_CORRUPT(grp))__builtin_expect(!!((((__builtin_constant_p(2) && __builtin_constant_p
((uintptr_t)(&((grp)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void *
)0)) && (uintptr_t)(&((grp)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t
)((void *)0) && __builtin_constant_p(*(const unsigned
long *)(&((grp)->bb_state)))) ? const_test_bit(2, &
((grp)->bb_state)) : _test_bit(2, &((grp)->bb_state
))))), 0))
2806 goto out;
2807 if (should_lock) {
2808 __acquire(ext4_group_lock_ptr(sb, group))do { } while (0);
2809 ext4_unlock_group(sb, group);
2810 }
2811
2812 /* We only do this if the grp has never been initialized */
2813 if (unlikely(EXT4_MB_GRP_NEED_INIT(grp))__builtin_expect(!!((((__builtin_constant_p(0) && __builtin_constant_p
((uintptr_t)(&((grp)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void *
)0)) && (uintptr_t)(&((grp)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t
)((void *)0) && __builtin_constant_p(*(const unsigned
long *)(&((grp)->bb_state)))) ? const_test_bit(0, &
((grp)->bb_state)) : _test_bit(0, &((grp)->bb_state
))))), 0)) {
2814 struct ext4_group_desc *gdp =
2815 ext4_get_group_desc(sb, group, NULL((void *)0));
2816 int ret;
2817
2818 /*
2819 * CR_POWER2_ALIGNED/CR_GOAL_LEN_FAST is a very optimistic
2820 * search to find large good chunks almost for free. If buddy
2821 * data is not ready, then this optimization makes no sense. But
2822 * we never skip the first block group in a flex_bg, since this
2823 * gets used for metadata block allocation, and we want to make
2824 * sure we locate metadata blocks in the first block group in
2825 * the flex_bg if possible.
2826 */
2827 if (!ext4_mb_cr_expensive(cr) &&
2828 (!sbi->s_log_groups_per_flex ||
2829 ((group & ((1 << sbi->s_log_groups_per_flex) - 1)) != 0)) &&
2830 !(ext4_has_group_desc_csum(sb) &&
2831 (gdp->bg_flags & cpu_to_le16(EXT4_BG_BLOCK_UNINIT)(( __le16)(__u16)(0x0002)))))
2832 return 0;
2833 ret = ext4_mb_init_group(sb, group, GFP_NOFS((( gfp_t)(((((1UL))) << (___GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM_BIT))|(
(((1UL))) << (___GFP_KSWAPD_RECLAIM_BIT)))) | (( gfp_t)
((((1UL))) << (___GFP_IO_BIT)))));
2834 if (ret)
2835 return ret;
2836 }
2837
2838 if (should_lock) {
2839 ext4_lock_group(sb, group);
2840 __release(ext4_group_lock_ptr(sb, group))do { } while (0);
2841 }
2842 ret = ext4_mb_good_group(ac, group, cr);
2843out:
2844 if (should_lock) {
2845 __acquire(ext4_group_lock_ptr(sb, group))do { } while (0);
2846 ext4_unlock_group(sb, group);
2847 }
2848 return ret;
2849}
2850
2851/*
2852 * Start prefetching @nr block bitmaps starting at @group.
2853 * Return the next group which needs to be prefetched.
2854 */
2855ext4_group_t ext4_mb_prefetch(struct super_block *sb, ext4_group_t group,
2856 unsigned int nr, int *cnt)
2857{
2858 ext4_group_t ngroups = ext4_get_groups_count(sb);
2859 struct buffer_head *bh;
2860 struct blk_plug plug;
2861
2862 blk_start_plug(&plug);
2863 while (nr-- > 0) {
2864 struct ext4_group_desc *gdp = ext4_get_group_desc(sb, group,
2865 NULL((void *)0));
2866 struct ext4_group_info *grp = ext4_get_group_info(sb, group);
2867
2868 /*
2869 * Prefetch block groups with free blocks; but don't
2870 * bother if it is marked uninitialized on disk, since
2871 * it won't require I/O to read. Also only try to
2872 * prefetch once, so we avoid getblk() call, which can
2873 * be expensive.
2874 */
2875 if (gdp && grp && !EXT4_MB_GRP_TEST_AND_SET_READ(grp)(test_and_set_bit(4, &((grp)->bb_state))) &&
2876 EXT4_MB_GRP_NEED_INIT(grp)(((__builtin_constant_p(0) && __builtin_constant_p((uintptr_t
)(&((grp)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void *)0)) &&
(uintptr_t)(&((grp)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void *
)0) && __builtin_constant_p(*(const unsigned long *)(
&((grp)->bb_state)))) ? const_test_bit(0, &((grp)->
bb_state)) : _test_bit(0, &((grp)->bb_state)))) &&
2877 ext4_free_group_clusters(sb, gdp) > 0 ) {
2878 bh = ext4_read_block_bitmap_nowait(sb, group, true);
2879 if (!IS_ERR_OR_NULL(bh)) {
2880 if (!buffer_uptodate(bh) && cnt)
2881 (*cnt)++;
2882 brelse(bh);
2883 }
2884 }
2885 if (++group >= ngroups)
2886 group = 0;
2887 }
2888 blk_finish_plug(&plug);
2889 return group;
2890}
2891
2892/*
2893 * Batch reads of the block allocation bitmaps to get
2894 * multiple READs in flight; limit prefetching at inexpensive
2895 * CR, otherwise mballoc can spend a lot of time loading
2896 * imperfect groups
2897 */
2898static void ext4_mb_might_prefetch(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
2899 ext4_group_t group)
2900{
2901 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi;

2903 if (ac->ac_prefetch_grp != group)
2904 return;
2905
2906 sbi = EXT4_SB(ac->ac_sb);
2907 if (ext4_mb_cr_expensive(ac->ac_criteria) ||
2908 ac->ac_prefetch_ios < sbi->s_mb_prefetch_limit) {
2909 unsigned int nr = sbi->s_mb_prefetch;
2910
2911 if (ext4_has_feature_flex_bg(ac->ac_sb)) {
2912 nr = 1 << sbi->s_log_groups_per_flex;
2913 nr -= group & (nr - 1);
2914 nr = umin(nr, sbi->s_mb_prefetch)({ __auto_type __UNIQUE_ID_x__1645 = ((nr) + 0u + 0ul + 0ull)
; __auto_type __UNIQUE_ID_y__1646 = ((sbi->s_mb_prefetch) +
0u + 0ul + 0ull); do { __attribute__((__noreturn__)) extern void
__compiletime_assert_1647(void) __attribute__((__error__("min"
"(""(nr) + 0u + 0ul + 0ull"", ""(sbi->s_mb_prefetch) + 0u + 0ul + 0ull"
") signedness error"))); if (!(!(!(((((typeof(__UNIQUE_ID_x__1645
))(-1)) < ( typeof(__UNIQUE_ID_x__1645))1) ? (2 + (__builtin_constant_p

(long long)(__UNIQUE_ID_y__1646) >= 0) && ((long long

) < 4))))))) __compiletime_assert_1647(); } while (0); ((__UNIQUE_ID_x__1645

2916
2917 ac->ac_prefetch_nr = nr;

2919 &ac->ac_prefetch_ios);

2921}

2923/*
2924 * Prefetching reads the block bitmap into the buffer cache; but we
2925 * need to make sure that the buddy bitmap in the page cache has been
2926 * initialized. Note that ext4_mb_init_group() will block if the I/O
2927 * is not yet completed, or indeed if it was not initiated by
2928 * ext4_mb_prefetch did not start the I/O.
2929 *
2930 * TODO: We should actually kick off the buddy bitmap setup in a work
2931 * queue when the buffer I/O is completed, so that we don't block
2932 * waiting for the block allocation bitmap read to finish when
2933 * ext4_mb_prefetch_fini is called from ext4_mb_regular_allocator().
2934 */
2935void ext4_mb_prefetch_fini(struct super_block *sb, ext4_group_t group,
2936 unsigned int nr)
2937{
2938 struct ext4_group_desc *gdp;
2939 struct ext4_group_info *grp;
2940
2941 while (nr-- > 0) {
2942 if (!group)
2943 group = ext4_get_groups_count(sb);
2944 group--;
2945 gdp = ext4_get_group_desc(sb, group, NULL((void *)0));
2946 grp = ext4_get_group_info(sb, group);
2947
2948 if (grp && gdp && EXT4_MB_GRP_NEED_INIT(grp)(((__builtin_constant_p(0) && __builtin_constant_p((uintptr_t
)(&((grp)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void *)0)) &&
(uintptr_t)(&((grp)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void *
)0) && __builtin_constant_p(*(const unsigned long *)(
&((grp)->bb_state)))) ? const_test_bit(0, &((grp)->
bb_state)) : _test_bit(0, &((grp)->bb_state)))) &&
2949 ext4_free_group_clusters(sb, gdp) > 0) {
2950 if (ext4_mb_init_group(sb, group, GFP_NOFS((( gfp_t)(((((1UL))) << (___GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM_BIT))|(
(((1UL))) << (___GFP_KSWAPD_RECLAIM_BIT)))) | (( gfp_t)
((((1UL))) << (___GFP_IO_BIT))))))
2951 break;
2952 }
2953 }
2954}
2955
2956static int ext4_mb_scan_group(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
2957 ext4_group_t group)
2958{
2959 int ret;
2960 struct super_block *sb = ac->ac_sb;
2961 enum criteria cr = ac->ac_criteria;
2962
2963 ext4_mb_might_prefetch(ac, group);
2964
2965 /* prevent unnecessary buddy loading. */
2966 if (cr < CR_ANY_FREE && spin_is_locked(ext4_group_lock_ptr(sb, group)))
2967 return 0;
2968
2969 /* This now checks without needing the buddy folio */
2970 ret = ext4_mb_good_group_nolock(ac, group, cr);
2971 if (ret <= 0) {
2972 if (!ac->ac_first_err)
2973 ac->ac_first_err = ret;
2974 return 0;
2975 }
2976
2977 ret = ext4_mb_load_buddy(sb, group, ac->ac_e4b);
2978 if (ret)
2979 return ret;
2980
2981 /* skip busy group */
2982 if (cr >= CR_ANY_FREE)
2983 ext4_lock_group(sb, group);
2984 else if (!ext4_try_lock_group(sb, group))
2985 goto out_unload;
2986
2987 /* We need to check again after locking the block group. */
2988 if (unlikely(!ext4_mb_good_group(ac, group, cr))__builtin_expect(!!(!ext4_mb_good_group(ac, group, cr)), 0))
2989 goto out_unlock;
2990
2991 __ext4_mb_scan_group(ac);
2992
2993out_unlock:
2994 ext4_unlock_group(sb, group);
2995out_unload:
2996 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(ac->ac_e4b);
2997 return ret;
2998}
2999

[hash])); });
3044 ac->ac_g_ex.fe_start = -1;
3045 ac->ac_flags &= ~EXT4_MB_HINT_TRY_GOAL0x0200;
3046 }
3047
3048 /*
3049 * Let's just scan groups to find more-less suitable blocks We
3050 * start with CR_GOAL_LEN_FAST, unless it is power of 2
3051 * aligned, in which case let's do that faster approach first.
3052 */
3053 ac->ac_criteria = CR_GOAL_LEN_FAST;
3054 if (ac->ac_2order)
3055 ac->ac_criteria = CR_POWER2_ALIGNED;
3056
3057 ac->ac_e4b = &e4b;
3058 ac->ac_prefetch_ios = 0;
3059 ac->ac_first_err = 0;
3060repeat:

3062 err = ext4_mb_scan_groups(ac);
3063 if (err)
3064 goto out;
3065
3066 if (ac->ac_status != AC_STATUS_CONTINUE1)
3067 break;
3068 }
3069
3070 if (ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len > 0 && ac->ac_status != AC_STATUS_FOUND2 &&
3071 !(ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_FIRST0x0008)) {
3072 /*
3073 * We've been searching too long. Let's try to allocate
3074 * the best chunk we've found so far
3075 */
3076 ext4_mb_try_best_found(ac, &e4b);
3077 if (ac->ac_status != AC_STATUS_FOUND2) {
3078 int lost;
3079
3080 /*
3081 * Someone more lucky has already allocated it.
3082 * The only thing we can do is just take first
3083 * found block(s)
3084 */
3085 lost = atomic_inc_return(&sbi->s_mb_lost_chunks);
3086 mb_debug(sb, "lost chunk, group: %u, start: %d, len: %d, lost: %d\n",({ if (0) _printk("lost chunk, group: %u, start: %d, len: %d, lost: %d\n"
, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_group, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_start, ac->ac_b_ex
.fe_len, lost); 0; })
3087 ac->ac_b_ex.fe_group, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_start,({ if (0) _printk("lost chunk, group: %u, start: %d, len: %d, lost: %d\n"
, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_group, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_start, ac->ac_b_ex
.fe_len, lost); 0; })
3088 ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len, lost)({ if (0) _printk("lost chunk, group: %u, start: %d, len: %d, lost: %d\n"
, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_group, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_start, ac->ac_b_ex
.fe_len, lost); 0; });
3089
3090 ac->ac_b_ex.fe_group = 0;
3091 ac->ac_b_ex.fe_start = 0;
3092 ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len = 0;
3093 ac->ac_status = AC_STATUS_CONTINUE1;
3094 ac->ac_flags |= EXT4_MB_HINT_FIRST0x0008;
3095 ac->ac_criteria = CR_ANY_FREE;
3096 goto repeat;
3097 }
3098 }
3099
3100 if (sbi->s_mb_stats && ac->ac_status == AC_STATUS_FOUND2) {
3101 atomic64_inc(&sbi->s_bal_cX_hits[ac->ac_criteria]);
3102 if (ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_STREAM_ALLOC0x0800 &&
3103 ac->ac_b_ex.fe_group == ac->ac_g_ex.fe_group)
3104 atomic_inc(&sbi->s_bal_stream_goals);
3105 }
3106out:
3107 if (!err && ac->ac_status != AC_STATUS_FOUND2 && ac->ac_first_err)
3108 err = ac->ac_first_err;
3109
3110 mb_debug(sb, "Best len %d, origin len %d, ac_status %u, ac_flags 0x%x, cr %d ret %d\n",({ if (0) _printk("Best len %d, origin len %d, ac_status %u, ac_flags 0x%x, cr %d ret %d\n"
, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len, ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len, ac->ac_status
, ac->ac_flags, ac->ac_criteria, err); 0; })
3111 ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len, ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len, ac->ac_status,({ if (0) _printk("Best len %d, origin len %d, ac_status %u, ac_flags 0x%x, cr %d ret %d\n"
, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len, ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len, ac->ac_status
, ac->ac_flags, ac->ac_criteria, err); 0; })
3112 ac->ac_flags, ac->ac_criteria, err)({ if (0) _printk("Best len %d, origin len %d, ac_status %u, ac_flags 0x%x, cr %d ret %d\n"
, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len, ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len, ac->ac_status
, ac->ac_flags, ac->ac_criteria, err); 0; });
3113
3114 if (ac->ac_prefetch_nr)
3115 ext4_mb_prefetch_fini(sb, ac->ac_prefetch_grp, ac->ac_prefetch_nr);
3116
3117 return err;
3118}
3119
3120static void *ext4_mb_seq_groups_start(struct seq_file *seq, loff_t *pos)
3121{
3122 struct super_block *sb = pde_data(file_inode(seq->file));
3123 ext4_group_t group;
3124
3125 if (*pos < 0 || *pos >= ext4_get_groups_count(sb))
3126 return NULL((void *)0);
3127 group = *pos + 1;
3128 return (void *) ((unsigned long) group);
3129}
3130
3131static void *ext4_mb_seq_groups_next(struct seq_file *seq, void *v, loff_t *pos)
3132{
3133 struct super_block *sb = pde_data(file_inode(seq->file));
3134 ext4_group_t group;
3135
3136 ++*pos;
3137 if (*pos < 0 || *pos >= ext4_get_groups_count(sb))
3138 return NULL((void *)0);
3139 group = *pos + 1;
3140 return (void *) ((unsigned long) group);
3141}

(!(!__builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof((((struct ext4_group_info
*)((void *)0))->bb_counters)), typeof(&(((struct ext4_group_info
*)((void *)0))->bb_counters)[0])))), "must be array");}))
)), size_add(sizeof(*((struct ext4_group_info *)((void *)0)))
, __builtin_choose_expr((sizeof(int) == sizeof(*(8 ? ((void *
)((long)(16 + 2) * 0l)) : (int *)8))), (16 + 2) * sizeof(*((struct
ext4_group_info *)((void *)0))->bb_counters) + ((int)sizeof
(struct {_Static_assert(!(!(!__builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof
((((struct ext4_group_info *)((void *)0))->bb_counters)), typeof
(&(((struct ext4_group_info *)((void *)0))->bb_counters
)[0])))), "must be array");})), size_mul(16 + 2, sizeof(*((struct
ext4_group_info *)((void *)0))->bb_counters) + ((int)sizeof
(struct {_Static_assert(!(!(!__builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof
((((struct ext4_group_info *)((void *)0))->bb_counters)), typeof
(&(((struct ext4_group_info *)((void *)0))->bb_counters
)[0])))), "must be array");}))))))]; struct ext4_group_info obj
; } sg_u = { }; struct ext4_group_info *sg = (struct ext4_group_info
*)&sg_u;
3156
3157 group--;
3158 if (group == 0)
3159 seq_puts(seq, "#group: free frags first ["
3160 " 2^0 2^1 2^2 2^3 2^4 2^5 2^6 "
3161 " 2^7 2^8 2^9 2^10 2^11 2^12 2^13 ]\n");
3162
3163 i = (blocksize_bits + 2) * sizeof(sg->bb_counters[0]) +
3164 sizeof(struct ext4_group_info);
3165
3166 grinfo = ext4_get_group_info(sb, group);
3167 if (!grinfo)
3168 return 0;
3169 /* Load the group info in memory only if not already loaded. */
3170 if (unlikely(EXT4_MB_GRP_NEED_INIT(grinfo))__builtin_expect(!!((((__builtin_constant_p(0) && __builtin_constant_p
((uintptr_t)(&((grinfo)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void
*)0)) && (uintptr_t)(&((grinfo)->bb_state)) !=
(uintptr_t)((void *)0) && __builtin_constant_p(*(const
unsigned long *)(&((grinfo)->bb_state)))) ? const_test_bit
(0, &((grinfo)->bb_state)) : _test_bit(0, &((grinfo
)->bb_state))))), 0)) {
3171 err = ext4_mb_load_buddy(sb, group, &e4b);
3172 if (err) {
3173 seq_printf(seq, "#%-5u: %s\n", group, ext4_decode_error(NULL((void *)0), err, nbuf));
3174 return 0;
3175 }
3176 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(&e4b);
3177 }
3178
3179 /*
3180 * We care only about free space counters in the group info and
3181 * these are safe to access even after the buddy has been unloaded
3182 */
3183 memcpy(sg, grinfo, i);
3184 seq_printf(seq, "#%-5u: %-5u %-5u %-5u [", group, sg->bb_free,
3185 sg->bb_fragments, sg->bb_first_free);
3186 for (i = 0; i <= 13; i++)
3187 seq_printf(seq, " %-5u", i <= blocksize_bits + 1 ?
3188 sg->bb_counters[i] : 0);
3189 seq_puts(seq, " ]");
3190 if (EXT4_MB_GRP_BBITMAP_CORRUPT(sg)(((__builtin_constant_p(2) && __builtin_constant_p((uintptr_t
)(&((sg)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void *)0)) &&
(uintptr_t)(&((sg)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void *
)0) && __builtin_constant_p(*(const unsigned long *)(
&((sg)->bb_state)))) ? const_test_bit(2, &((sg)->
bb_state)) : _test_bit(2, &((sg)->bb_state)))))
3191 seq_puts(seq, " Block bitmap corrupted!");
3192 seq_putc(seq, '\n');
3193 return 0;
3194}
3195
3196static void ext4_mb_seq_groups_stop(struct seq_file *seq, void *v)
3197{
3198}
3199
3200const struct seq_operations ext4_mb_seq_groups_ops = {
3201 .start = ext4_mb_seq_groups_start,
3202 .next = ext4_mb_seq_groups_next,
3203 .stop = ext4_mb_seq_groups_stop,
3204 .show = ext4_mb_seq_groups_show,
3205};
3206
3207int ext4_seq_mb_stats_show(struct seq_file *seq, void *offset)
3208{
3209 struct super_block *sb = seq->private;
3210 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(sb);
3211
3212 seq_puts(seq, "mballoc:\n");
3213 if (!sbi->s_mb_stats) {
3214 seq_puts(seq, "\tmb stats collection turned off.\n");
3215 seq_puts(
3216 seq,
3217 "\tTo enable, please write \"1\" to sysfs file mb_stats.\n");
3218 return 0;
3219 }
3220 seq_printf(seq, "\treqs: %u\n", atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_reqs));
3221 seq_printf(seq, "\tsuccess: %u\n", atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_success));
3222
3223 seq_printf(seq, "\tgroups_scanned: %u\n",
3224 atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_groups_scanned));
3225
3226 /* CR_POWER2_ALIGNED stats */
3227 seq_puts(seq, "\tcr_p2_aligned_stats:\n");
3228 seq_printf(seq, "\t\thits: %llu\n",
3229 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_hits[CR_POWER2_ALIGNED]));
3230 seq_printf(
3231 seq, "\t\tgroups_considered: %llu\n",
3232 atomic64_read(
3233 &sbi->s_bal_cX_groups_considered[CR_POWER2_ALIGNED]));
3234 seq_printf(seq, "\t\textents_scanned: %u\n",
3235 atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_ex_scanned[CR_POWER2_ALIGNED]));
3236 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tuseless_loops: %llu\n",
3237 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_failed[CR_POWER2_ALIGNED]));
3238
3239 /* CR_GOAL_LEN_FAST stats */
3240 seq_puts(seq, "\tcr_goal_fast_stats:\n");
3241 seq_printf(seq, "\t\thits: %llu\n",
3242 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_hits[CR_GOAL_LEN_FAST]));
3243 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tgroups_considered: %llu\n",
3244 atomic64_read(
3245 &sbi->s_bal_cX_groups_considered[CR_GOAL_LEN_FAST]));
3246 seq_printf(seq, "\t\textents_scanned: %u\n",
3247 atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_ex_scanned[CR_GOAL_LEN_FAST]));
3248 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tuseless_loops: %llu\n",
3249 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_failed[CR_GOAL_LEN_FAST]));
3250
3251 /* CR_BEST_AVAIL_LEN stats */
3252 seq_puts(seq, "\tcr_best_avail_stats:\n");
3253 seq_printf(seq, "\t\thits: %llu\n",
3254 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_hits[CR_BEST_AVAIL_LEN]));
3255 seq_printf(
3256 seq, "\t\tgroups_considered: %llu\n",
3257 atomic64_read(
3258 &sbi->s_bal_cX_groups_considered[CR_BEST_AVAIL_LEN]));
3259 seq_printf(seq, "\t\textents_scanned: %u\n",
3260 atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_ex_scanned[CR_BEST_AVAIL_LEN]));
3261 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tuseless_loops: %llu\n",
3262 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_failed[CR_BEST_AVAIL_LEN]));
3263
3264 /* CR_GOAL_LEN_SLOW stats */
3265 seq_puts(seq, "\tcr_goal_slow_stats:\n");
3266 seq_printf(seq, "\t\thits: %llu\n",
3267 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_hits[CR_GOAL_LEN_SLOW]));
3268 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tgroups_considered: %llu\n",
3269 atomic64_read(
3270 &sbi->s_bal_cX_groups_considered[CR_GOAL_LEN_SLOW]));
3271 seq_printf(seq, "\t\textents_scanned: %u\n",
3272 atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_ex_scanned[CR_GOAL_LEN_SLOW]));
3273 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tuseless_loops: %llu\n",
3274 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_failed[CR_GOAL_LEN_SLOW]));
3275
3276 /* CR_ANY_FREE stats */
3277 seq_puts(seq, "\tcr_any_free_stats:\n");
3278 seq_printf(seq, "\t\thits: %llu\n",
3279 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_hits[CR_ANY_FREE]));
3280 seq_printf(
3281 seq, "\t\tgroups_considered: %llu\n",
3282 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_groups_considered[CR_ANY_FREE]));
3283 seq_printf(seq, "\t\textents_scanned: %u\n",
3284 atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_ex_scanned[CR_ANY_FREE]));
3285 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tuseless_loops: %llu\n",
3286 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_bal_cX_failed[CR_ANY_FREE]));
3287
3288 /* Aggregates */
3289 seq_printf(seq, "\textents_scanned: %u\n",
3290 atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_ex_scanned));
3291 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tgoal_hits: %u\n", atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_goals));
3292 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tstream_goal_hits: %u\n",
3293 atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_stream_goals));
3294 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tlen_goal_hits: %u\n",
3295 atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_len_goals));
3296 seq_printf(seq, "\t\t2^n_hits: %u\n", atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_2orders));
3297 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tbreaks: %u\n", atomic_read(&sbi->s_bal_breaks));
3298 seq_printf(seq, "\t\tlost: %u\n", atomic_read(&sbi->s_mb_lost_chunks));
3299 seq_printf(seq, "\tbuddies_generated: %u/%u\n",
3300 atomic_read(&sbi->s_mb_buddies_generated),
3301 ext4_get_groups_count(sb));
3302 seq_printf(seq, "\tbuddies_time_used: %llu\n",
3303 atomic64_read(&sbi->s_mb_generation_time));
3304 seq_printf(seq, "\tpreallocated: %u\n",
3305 atomic_read(&sbi->s_mb_preallocated));
3306 seq_printf(seq, "\tdiscarded: %u\n", atomic_read(&sbi->s_mb_discarded));
3307 return 0;
3308}
3309
3310static void *ext4_mb_seq_structs_summary_start(struct seq_file *seq, loff_t *pos)
3311{
3312 struct super_block *sb = pde_data(file_inode(seq->file));
3313 unsigned long position;
3314
3315 if (*pos < 0 || *pos >= 2*MB_NUM_ORDERS(sb)((sb)->s_blocksize_bits + 2))
3316 return NULL((void *)0);
3317 position = *pos + 1;
3318 return (void *) ((unsigned long) position);
3319}
3320
3321static void *ext4_mb_seq_structs_summary_next(struct seq_file *seq, void *v, loff_t *pos)
3322{
3323 struct super_block *sb = pde_data(file_inode(seq->file));
3324 unsigned long position;
3325
3326 ++*pos;
3327 if (*pos < 0 || *pos >= 2*MB_NUM_ORDERS(sb)((sb)->s_blocksize_bits + 2))
3328 return NULL((void *)0);
3329 position = *pos + 1;
3330 return (void *) ((unsigned long) position);
3331}
3332
3333static int ext4_mb_seq_structs_summary_show(struct seq_file *seq, void *v)
3334{
3335 struct super_block *sb = pde_data(file_inode(seq->file));
3336 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(sb);
3337 unsigned long position = ((unsigned long) v);
3338 struct ext4_group_info *grp;
3339 unsigned int count;
3340 unsigned long idx;
3341
3342 position--;
3343 if (position >= MB_NUM_ORDERS(sb)((sb)->s_blocksize_bits + 2)) {
3344 position -= MB_NUM_ORDERS(sb)((sb)->s_blocksize_bits + 2);
3345 if (position == 0)
3346 seq_puts(seq, "avg_fragment_size_lists:\n");
3347
3348 count = 0;
3349 xa_for_each(&sbi->s_mb_avg_fragment_size[position], idx, grp)for (idx = 0, grp = xa_find(&sbi->s_mb_avg_fragment_size
[position], &idx, (~0UL), (( xa_mark_t)8U)); grp; grp = xa_find_after
(&sbi->s_mb_avg_fragment_size[position], &idx, (~0UL
), (( xa_mark_t)8U)))
3350 count++;
3351 seq_printf(seq, "\tlist_order_%u_groups: %u\n",
3352 (unsigned int)position, count);
3353 return 0;
3354 }
3355
3356 if (position == 0) {
3357 seq_printf(seq, "optimize_scan: %d\n",
3358 test_opt2(sb, MB_OPTIMIZE_SCAN)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_mount_opt2 & 0x00000080) ? 1 : 0);
3359 seq_puts(seq, "max_free_order_lists:\n");
3360 }
3361 count = 0;
3362 xa_for_each(&sbi->s_mb_largest_free_orders[position], idx, grp)for (idx = 0, grp = xa_find(&sbi->s_mb_largest_free_orders
[position], &idx, (~0UL), (( xa_mark_t)8U)); grp; grp = xa_find_after
(&sbi->s_mb_largest_free_orders[position], &idx, (
~0UL), (( xa_mark_t)8U)))
3363 count++;
3364 seq_printf(seq, "\tlist_order_%u_groups: %u\n",
3365 (unsigned int)position, count);
3366
3367 return 0;
3368}
3369
3370static void ext4_mb_seq_structs_summary_stop(struct seq_file *seq, void *v)
3371{
3372}
3373
3374const struct seq_operations ext4_mb_seq_structs_summary_ops = {
3375 .start = ext4_mb_seq_structs_summary_start,
3376 .next = ext4_mb_seq_structs_summary_next,
3377 .stop = ext4_mb_seq_structs_summary_stop,
3378 .show = ext4_mb_seq_structs_summary_show,
3379};
3380
3381static struct kmem_cache *get_groupinfo_cache(int blocksize_bits)
3382{
3383 int cache_index = blocksize_bits - EXT4_MIN_BLOCK_LOG_SIZE10;
3384 struct kmem_cache *cachep = ext4_groupinfo_caches[cache_index];
3385
3386 BUG_ON(!cachep)do { if (__builtin_expect(!!(!cachep), 0)) do { ({ asm volatile
("1654" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1654b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1654)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" "" : : [fmt] "i"
(""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line] "i" (3386), [
fl] "i" (0), [size] "i" (sizeof(struct bug_entry))); } while (
0); __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0); } while (0);
3387 return cachep;
3388}
3389
3390/*
3391 * Allocate the top-level s_group_info array for the specified number
3392 * of groups
3393 */

(( __builtin_constant_p((sizeof(*sbi->s_group_info) * size
) - 1) ? (((sizeof(*sbi->s_group_info) * size) - 1) < 2

<= 4) ? __ilog2_u32((sizeof(*sbi->s_group_info) * size
) - 1) : __ilog2_u64((sizeof(*sbi->s_group_info) * size) -
1) ) + 1)) ) : __roundup_pow_of_two(sizeof(*sbi->s_group_info
) * size) );

((((1UL))) << (___GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM_BIT))|((((1UL))) <<
(___GFP_KSWAPD_RECLAIM_BIT)))) | (( gfp_t)((((1UL))) <<
(___GFP_IO_BIT))) | (( gfp_t)((((1UL))) << (___GFP_FS_BIT
)))))|(( gfp_t)((((1UL))) << (___GFP_ZERO_BIT))), (-1))

3425
3426/* Create and initialize ext4_group_info data for the given group. */
3427int ext4_mb_add_groupinfo(struct super_block *sb, ext4_group_t group,
3428 struct ext4_group_desc *desc)
3429{
3430 int i;
3431 int metalen = 0;
3432 int idx = group >> EXT4_DESC_PER_BLOCK_BITS(sb)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_desc_per_block_bits);
3433 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(sb);
3434 struct ext4_group_info **meta_group_info;
3435 struct kmem_cache *cachep = get_groupinfo_cache(sb->s_blocksize_bits);

discard_list)); fd = nfd, nfd = ({ void *__mptr = (void *)((nfd
)->efd_list.next); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*((nfd)->efd_list.next)), typeof(((typeof(*(nfd)) *
)0)->efd_list)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*((
nfd)->efd_list.next)), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"
); ((typeof(*(nfd)) *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(typeof(*(nfd
)), efd_list))); })) {
3659 /*
3660 * If filesystem is umounting or no memory or suffering
3661 * from no space, give up the discard
3662 */
3663 if ((sb->s_flags & SB_ACTIVE((((1UL))) << (30))) && !err &&
3664 !atomic_read(&sbi->s_retry_alloc_pending)) {
3665 grp = fd->efd_group;
3666 if (grp != load_grp) {
3667 if (load_grp != UINT_MAX(~0U))
3668 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(&e4b);
3669
3670 err = ext4_mb_load_buddy(sb, grp, &e4b);
3671 if (err) {
3672 kmem_cache_free(ext4_free_data_cachep, fd);
3673 load_grp = UINT_MAX(~0U);
3674 continue;
3675 } else {
3676 load_grp = grp;
3677 }
3678 }
3679
3680 ext4_lock_group(sb, grp);
3681 ext4_try_to_trim_range(sb, &e4b, fd->efd_start_cluster,
3682 fd->efd_start_cluster + fd->efd_count - 1, 1);
3683 ext4_unlock_group(sb, grp);
3684 }
3685 kmem_cache_free(ext4_free_data_cachep, fd);
3686 }
3687
3688 if (load_grp != UINT_MAX(~0U))
3689 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(&e4b);
3690}
3691
3692static inlineinline __attribute__((__gnu_inline__)) __attribute__((__unused__
)) __attribute__((no_instrument_function)) void ext4_mb_avg_fragment_size_destroy(struct ext4_sb_info *sbi)
3693{
3694 if (!sbi->s_mb_avg_fragment_size)
3695 return;
3696
3697 for (int i = 0; i < MB_NUM_ORDERS(sbi->s_sb)((sbi->s_sb)->s_blocksize_bits + 2); i++)
3698 xa_destroy(&sbi->s_mb_avg_fragment_size[i]);
3699
3700 kfree(sbi->s_mb_avg_fragment_size);
3701 sbi->s_mb_avg_fragment_size = NULL((void *)0);
3702}
3703
3704static inlineinline __attribute__((__gnu_inline__)) __attribute__((__unused__
)) __attribute__((no_instrument_function)) void ext4_mb_largest_free_orders_destroy(struct ext4_sb_info *sbi)
3705{
3706 if (!sbi->s_mb_largest_free_orders)
3707 return;
3708
3709 for (int i = 0; i < MB_NUM_ORDERS(sbi->s_sb)((sbi->s_sb)->s_blocksize_bits + 2); i++)
3710 xa_destroy(&sbi->s_mb_largest_free_orders[i]);
3711
3712 kfree(sbi->s_mb_largest_free_orders);
3713 sbi->s_mb_largest_free_orders = NULL((void *)0);
3714}

3793 /*
3794 * The default group preallocation is 512, which for 4k block
3795 * sizes translates to 2 megabytes. However for bigalloc file
3796 * systems, this is probably too big (i.e, if the cluster size
3797 * is 1 megabyte, then group preallocation size becomes half a
3798 * gigabyte!). As a default, we will keep a two megabyte
3799 * group pralloc size for cluster sizes up to 64k, and after
3800 * that, we will force a minimum group preallocation size of
3801 * 32 clusters. This translates to 8 megs when the cluster
3802 * size is 256k, and 32 megs when the cluster size is 1 meg,
3803 * which seems reasonable as a default.

3854out_free_locality_groups:

3865 kfree(sbi->s_mb_maxs);
3866 sbi->s_mb_maxs = NULL((void *)0);
3867 return ret;
3868}

3870/* need to called with the ext4 group lock held */
3871static int ext4_mb_cleanup_pa(struct ext4_group_info *grp)
3872{
3873 struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa;
3874 struct list_head *cur, *tmp;
3875 int count = 0;
3876
3877 list_for_each_safe(cur, tmp, &grp->bb_prealloc_list)for (cur = (&grp->bb_prealloc_list)->next, tmp = cur
->next; !list_is_head(cur, (&grp->bb_prealloc_list)
); cur = tmp, tmp = cur->next) {
3878 pa = list_entry(cur, struct ext4_prealloc_space, pa_group_list)({ void *__mptr = (void *)(cur); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(cur)), typeof(((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)0)->
pa_group_list)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*(cur)
), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()");
((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(
struct ext4_prealloc_space, pa_group_list))); });
3879 list_del(&pa->pa_group_list);
3880 count++;
3881 kmem_cache_free(ext4_pspace_cachep, pa);
3882 }
3883 return count;
3884}

3959static inlineinline __attribute__((__gnu_inline__)) __attribute__((__unused__
)) __attribute__((no_instrument_function)) int ext4_issue_discard(struct super_block *sb,
3960 ext4_group_t block_group, ext4_grpblk_t cluster, int count)
3961{
3962 ext4_fsblk_t discard_block;
3963
3964 discard_block = (EXT4_C2B(EXT4_SB(sb), cluster)((cluster) << (EXT4_SB(sb))->s_cluster_bits) +
3965 ext4_group_first_block_no(sb, block_group));
3966 count = EXT4_C2B(EXT4_SB(sb), count)((count) << (EXT4_SB(sb))->s_cluster_bits);
3967 trace_ext4_discard_blocks(sb,
3968 (unsigned long long) discard_block, count);
3969
3970 return sb_issue_discard(sb, discard_block, count, GFP_NOFS((( gfp_t)(((((1UL))) << (___GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM_BIT))|(
(((1UL))) << (___GFP_KSWAPD_RECLAIM_BIT)))) | (( gfp_t)
((((1UL))) << (___GFP_IO_BIT)))), 0);
3971}
3972
3973static void ext4_free_data_in_buddy(struct super_block *sb,
3974 struct ext4_free_data *entry)
3975{
3976 struct ext4_buddy e4b;
3977 struct ext4_group_info *db;
3978 int err, count = 0;
3979
3980 mb_debug(sb, "gonna free %u blocks in group %u (0x%p):",({ if (0) _printk("gonna free %u blocks in group %u (0x%p):",
entry->efd_count, entry->efd_group, entry); 0; })
3981 entry->efd_count, entry->efd_group, entry)({ if (0) _printk("gonna free %u blocks in group %u (0x%p):",
entry->efd_count, entry->efd_group, entry); 0; });
3982
3983 err = ext4_mb_load_buddy(sb, entry->efd_group, &e4b);
3984 /* we expect to find existing buddy because it's pinned */
3985 BUG_ON(err != 0)do { if (__builtin_expect(!!(err != 0), 0)) do { ({ asm volatile
("1682" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1682b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1682)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" "" : : [fmt] "i"
(""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line] "i" (3985), [
fl] "i" (0), [size] "i" (sizeof(struct bug_entry))); } while (
0); __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0); } while (0);
3986
3987 atomic_sub(entry->efd_count, &EXT4_SB(sb)->s_mb_free_pending);
3988 db = e4b.bd_info;
3989 /* there are blocks to put in buddy to make them really free */
3990 count += entry->efd_count;
3991 ext4_lock_group(sb, entry->efd_group);
3992 /* Take it out of per group rb tree */
3993 rb_erase(&entry->efd_node, &(db->bb_free_root));
3994 mb_free_blocks(NULL((void *)0), &e4b, entry->efd_start_cluster, entry->efd_count);
3995
3996 /*
3997 * Clear the trimmed flag for the group so that the next
3998 * ext4_trim_fs can trim it.
3999 */
4000 EXT4_MB_GRP_CLEAR_TRIMMED(db)(clear_bit(1, &((db)->bb_state)));
4001
4002 if (!db->bb_free_root.rb_node) {
4003 /* No more items in the per group rb tree
4004 * balance refcounts from ext4_mb_free_metadata()
4005 */
4006 folio_put(e4b.bd_buddy_folio);
4007 folio_put(e4b.bd_bitmap_folio);
4008 }
4009 ext4_unlock_group(sb, entry->efd_group);
4010 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(&e4b);
4011
4012 mb_debug(sb, "freed %d blocks in 1 structures\n", count)({ if (0) _printk("freed %d blocks in 1 structures\n", count)
; 0; });
4013}
4014
4015/*
4016 * This function is called by the jbd2 layer once the commit has finished,
4017 * so we know we can free the blocks that were released with that commit.
4018 */
4019void ext4_process_freed_data(struct super_block *sb, tid_t commit_tid)
4020{
4021 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(sb);
4022 struct ext4_free_data *entry, *tmp;
4023 LIST_HEAD(freed_data_list)struct list_head freed_data_list = { &(freed_data_list), &
(freed_data_list) };
4024 struct list_head *s_freed_head = &sbi->s_freed_data_list[commit_tid & 1];
4025 bool wake;
4026
4027 list_replace_init(s_freed_head, &freed_data_list);
4028
4029 list_for_each_entry(entry, &freed_data_list, efd_list)for (entry = ({ void *__mptr = (void *)((&freed_data_list
)->next); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof
(*((&freed_data_list)->next)), typeof(((typeof(*entry)
*)0)->efd_list)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*
((&freed_data_list)->next)), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"
); ((typeof(*entry) *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(typeof(*entry
), efd_list))); }); !list_is_head(&entry->efd_list, (&
freed_data_list)); entry = ({ void *__mptr = (void *)((entry)
->efd_list.next); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*((entry)->efd_list.next)), typeof(((typeof(*(entry
)) *)0)->efd_list)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof
(*((entry)->efd_list.next)), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"
); ((typeof(*(entry)) *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(typeof(*
(entry)), efd_list))); }))
4030 ext4_free_data_in_buddy(sb, entry);
4031
4032 if (test_opt(sb, DISCARD)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_mount_opt & 0x40000000)) {
4033 spin_lock(&sbi->s_md_lock);
4034 wake = list_empty(&sbi->s_discard_list);
4035 list_splice_tail(&freed_data_list, &sbi->s_discard_list);
4036 spin_unlock(&sbi->s_md_lock);
4037 if (wake)
4038 queue_work(system_dfl_wq, &sbi->s_discard_work);

ext4_allocation_context), &(struct kmem_cache_args) { .align

4071
4072void ext4_exit_mballoc(void)
4073{
4074 /*
4075 * Wait for completion of call_rcu()'s on ext4_pspace_cachep
4076 * before destroying the slab cache.
4077 */
4078 rcu_barrier();
4079 kmem_cache_destroy(ext4_pspace_cachep);
4080 kmem_cache_destroy(ext4_ac_cachep);
4081 kmem_cache_destroy(ext4_free_data_cachep);
4082 ext4_groupinfo_destroy_slabs();
4083}
4084
4085#define EXT4_MB_BITMAP_MARKED_CHECK0x0001 0x0001
4086#define EXT4_MB_SYNC_UPDATE0x0002 0x0002
4087int
4088ext4_mb_mark_context(handle_t *handle, struct super_block *sb, bool state,
4089 ext4_group_t group, ext4_grpblk_t blkoff,
4090 ext4_grpblk_t len, int flags, ext4_grpblk_t *ret_changed)
4091{
4092 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(sb);
4093 struct buffer_head *bitmap_bh = NULL((void *)0);
4094 struct ext4_group_desc *gdp;
4095 struct buffer_head *gdp_bh;
4096 int err;
4097 unsigned int i, already, changed = len;
4098
4099 KUNIT_STATIC_STUB_REDIRECT(ext4_mb_mark_context,do {} while (0)
4100 handle, sb, state, group, blkoff, len,do {} while (0)
4101 flags, ret_changed)do {} while (0);
4102
4103 if (ret_changed)
4104 *ret_changed = 0;
4105 bitmap_bh = ext4_read_block_bitmap(sb, group);
4106 if (IS_ERR(bitmap_bh))
4107 return PTR_ERR(bitmap_bh);
4108
4109 if (handle) {
4110 BUFFER_TRACE(bitmap_bh, "getting write access")do {} while (0);
4111 err = ext4_journal_get_write_access(handle, sb, bitmap_bh,__ext4_journal_get_write_access(__func__, 4112, (handle), (sb
), (bitmap_bh), (EXT4_JTR_NONE))
4112 EXT4_JTR_NONE)__ext4_journal_get_write_access(__func__, 4112, (handle), (sb
), (bitmap_bh), (EXT4_JTR_NONE));
4113 if (err)
4114 goto out_err;
4115 }
4116
4117 err = -EIO5;
4118 gdp = ext4_get_group_desc(sb, group, &gdp_bh);
4119 if (!gdp)
4120 goto out_err;
4121
4122 if (handle) {
4123 BUFFER_TRACE(gdp_bh, "get_write_access")do {} while (0);
4124 err = ext4_journal_get_write_access(handle, sb, gdp_bh,__ext4_journal_get_write_access(__func__, 4125, (handle), (sb
), (gdp_bh), (EXT4_JTR_NONE))
4125 EXT4_JTR_NONE)__ext4_journal_get_write_access(__func__, 4125, (handle), (sb
), (gdp_bh), (EXT4_JTR_NONE));
4126 if (err)
4127 goto out_err;
4128 }
4129
4130 ext4_lock_group(sb, group);
4131 if (ext4_has_group_desc_csum(sb) &&
4132 (gdp->bg_flags & cpu_to_le16(EXT4_BG_BLOCK_UNINIT)(( __le16)(__u16)(0x0002)))) {
4133 gdp->bg_flags &= cpu_to_le16(~EXT4_BG_BLOCK_UNINIT)(( __le16)(__u16)(~0x0002));
4134 ext4_free_group_clusters_set(sb, gdp,
4135 ext4_free_clusters_after_init(sb, group, gdp));
4136 }
4137
4138 if (flags & EXT4_MB_BITMAP_MARKED_CHECK0x0001) {
4139 already = 0;
4140 for (i = 0; i < len; i++)
4141 if (mb_test_bit(blkoff + i, bitmap_bh->b_data) ==
4142 state)
4143 already++;
4144 changed = len - already;
4145 }
4146
4147 if (state) {
4148 mb_set_bits(bitmap_bh->b_data, blkoff, len);
4149 ext4_free_group_clusters_set(sb, gdp,
4150 ext4_free_group_clusters(sb, gdp) - changed);
4151 } else {
4152 mb_clear_bits(bitmap_bh->b_data, blkoff, len);
4153 ext4_free_group_clusters_set(sb, gdp,
4154 ext4_free_group_clusters(sb, gdp) + changed);
4155 }
4156
4157 ext4_block_bitmap_csum_set(sb, gdp, bitmap_bh);
4158 ext4_group_desc_csum_set(sb, group, gdp);
4159 ext4_unlock_group(sb, group);
4160 if (ret_changed)
4161 *ret_changed = changed;
4162
4163 if (sbi->s_log_groups_per_flex) {
4164 ext4_group_t flex_group = ext4_flex_group(sbi, group);
4165 struct flex_groups *fg = sbi_array_rcu_deref(sbi,({ typeof(*((sbi)->s_flex_groups)) _v; rcu_read_lock(); _v
= ((typeof(_v)*)({ typeof(*((sbi)->s_flex_groups)) *__UNIQUE_ID_rcu_1683

4255/*
4256 * Idempotent helper for Ext4 fast commit replay path to set the state of
4257 * blocks in bitmaps and update counters.
4258 */
4259void ext4_mb_mark_bb(struct super_block *sb, ext4_fsblk_t block,
4260 int len, bool state)

4275 * span across more than one group. In that case we need to
4276 * get the corresponding group metadata to work with.
4277 * For this we have goto again loop.

)) extern void __compiletime_assert_1690(void) __attribute__(

) < 4))) & ((((typeof(__UNIQUE_ID_y__1689))(-1)) < (

(long long)(__UNIQUE_ID_y__1689) >= 0) && ((long long

) < 4))))))) __compiletime_assert_1690(); } while (0); ((__UNIQUE_ID_x__1688
) < (__UNIQUE_ID_y__1689) ? (__UNIQUE_ID_x__1688) : (__UNIQUE_ID_y__1689
)); });

)->s_cluster_bits);

4283 ext4_error(sb, "Marking blocks in system zone - "__ext4_error((sb), __func__, 4285, false, 0, 0, ("Marking blocks in system zone - "
"Block = %llu, len = %u"), block, thisgrp_len)

"Block = %llu, len = %u"), block, thisgrp_len)
4285 block, thisgrp_len)__ext4_error((sb), __func__, 4285, false, 0, 0, ("Marking blocks in system zone - "
"Block = %llu, len = %u"), block, thisgrp_len);

4303/*
4304 * here we normalize request for locality group
4305 * Group request are normalized to s_mb_group_prealloc, which goes to
4306 * s_strip if we set the same via mount option.
4307 * s_mb_group_prealloc can be configured via
4308 * /sys/fs/ext4/<partition>/mb_group_prealloc

" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"

"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" "" : : [fmt] "i"

4318 ac->ac_g_ex.fe_len = EXT4_SB(sb)->s_mb_group_prealloc;
4319 mb_debug(sb, "goal %u blocks for locality group\n", ac->ac_g_ex.fe_len)({ if (0) _printk("goal %u blocks for locality group\n", ac->
ac_g_ex.fe_len); 0; });

4321
4322/*
4323 * This function returns the next element to look at during inode
4324 * PA rbtree walk. We assume that we have held the inode PA rbtree lock
4325 * (ei->i_prealloc_lock)
4326 *
4327 * new_start The start of the range we want to compare
4328 * cur_start The existing start that we are comparing against
4329 * node The node of the rb_tree
4330 */
4331static inlineinline __attribute__((__gnu_inline__)) __attribute__((__unused__
)) __attribute__((no_instrument_function)) struct rb_node*
4332ext4_mb_pa_rb_next_iter(ext4_lblk_t new_start, ext4_lblk_t cur_start, struct rb_node *node)
4333{
4334 if (new_start < cur_start)
4335 return node->rb_left;
4336 else
4337 return node->rb_right;
4338}
4339

4367/*
4368 * Given an allocation context "ac" and a range "start", "end", check
4369 * and adjust boundaries if the range overlaps with any of the existing
4370 * preallocatoins stored in the corresponding inode of the allocation context.

4494 /* Step 4: trim our normalized range to not overlap with the neighbors */
4495 if (left_pa) {

4498 }

4507 ext4_mb_pa_assert_overlap(ac, new_start, new_end);
4508
4509 *start = new_start;
4510 *end = new_end;
4511}
4512
4513/*
4514 * Normalization means making request better in terms of
4515 * size and alignment
4516 */
4517static noinline_for_stack__attribute__((__noinline__)) void
4518ext4_mb_normalize_request(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
4519 struct ext4_allocation_request *ar)
4520{
4521 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(ac->ac_sb);
4522 struct ext4_super_block *es = sbi->s_es;
4523 int bsbits, max;
4524 loff_t size, start_off, end;
4525 loff_t orig_size __maybe_unused__attribute__((__unused__));
4526 ext4_lblk_t start;
4527
4528 /* do normalize only data requests, metadata requests
4529 do not need preallocation */
4530 if (!(ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_DATA0x0020))
4531 return;
4532
4533 /* sometime caller may want exact blocks */
4534 if (unlikely(ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_GOAL_ONLY)__builtin_expect(!!(ac->ac_flags & 0x0100), 0))
4535 return;
4536
4537 /* caller may indicate that preallocation isn't
4538 * required (it's a tail, for example) */
4539 if (ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_NOPREALLOC0x0040)
4540 return;
4541
4542 if (ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_GROUP_ALLOC0x0080) {
4543 ext4_mb_normalize_group_request(ac);
4544 return ;
4545 }
4546
4547 bsbits = ac->ac_sb->s_blocksize_bits;
4548
4549 /* first, let's learn actual file size
4550 * given current request is allocated */
4551 size = extent_logical_end(sbi, &ac->ac_o_ex);
4552 size = size << bsbits;
4553 if (size < i_size_read(ac->ac_inode))
4554 size = i_size_read(ac->ac_inode);
4555 orig_size = size;
4556
4557 /* max size of free chunks */
4558 max = 2 << bsbits;
4559
4560#define NRL_CHECK_SIZE(req, size, max, chunk_size)(req <= (size) || max <= (chunk_size)) \
4561 (req <= (size) || max <= (chunk_size))
4562
4563 /* first, try to predict filesize */
4564 start_off = 0;
4565 if (size <= SZ_1M0x00100000) {
4566 /*
4567 * For files up to 1MB, round up the preallocation size to
4568 * the next power of two, with a minimum of 16KB.
4569 */
4570 if (size <= (unsigned long)SZ_16K0x00004000)
4571 size = SZ_16K0x00004000;
4572 else
4573 size = roundup_pow_of_two(size)( __builtin_constant_p(size) ? ( ((size) == 1) ? 1 : (1UL <<
(( __builtin_constant_p((size) - 1) ? (((size) - 1) < 2 ?
0 : 63 - __builtin_clzll((size) - 1)) : (sizeof((size) - 1) <=
4) ? __ilog2_u32((size) - 1) : __ilog2_u64((size) - 1) ) + 1
)) ) : __roundup_pow_of_two(size) );
4574 } else if (NRL_CHECK_SIZE(size, 4 * 1024 * 1024, max, 2 * 1024)(size <= (4 * 1024 * 1024) || max <= (2 * 1024))) {
4575 start_off = ((loff_t)ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical >>
4576 (21 - bsbits)) << 21;
4577 size = 2 * 1024 * 1024;
4578 } else if (NRL_CHECK_SIZE(size, 8 * 1024 * 1024, max, 4 * 1024)(size <= (8 * 1024 * 1024) || max <= (4 * 1024))) {
4579 start_off = ((loff_t)ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical >>
4580 (22 - bsbits)) << 22;
4581 size = 4 * 1024 * 1024;
4582 } else if (NRL_CHECK_SIZE(EXT4_C2B(sbi, ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len),(((ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits) <=
((8<<20)>>bsbits) || max <= (8 * 1024))
4583 (8<<20)>>bsbits, max, 8 * 1024)(((ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits) <=
((8<<20)>>bsbits) || max <= (8 * 1024))) {

4585 (23 - bsbits)) << 23;
4586 size = 8 * 1024 * 1024;
4587 } else {
4588 start_off = (loff_t) ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical << bsbits;
4589 size = (loff_t) EXT4_C2B(sbi,((ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits)
4590 ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len)((ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits) << bsbits;
4591 }
4592 size = size >> bsbits;
4593 start = start_off >> bsbits;
4594

4597 * alignment may be larger than group size. Make sure the
4598 * alignment does not move allocation to a different group which
4599 * makes mballoc fail assertions later.

4603
4604 /* avoid unnecessary preallocation that may trigger assertions */
4605 if (start + size > EXT_MAX_BLOCKS0xffffffff)
4606 size = EXT_MAX_BLOCKS0xffffffff - start;
4607
4608 /* don't cover already allocated blocks in selected range */
4609 if (ar->pleft && start <= ar->lleft) {
4610 size -= ar->lleft + 1 - start;
4611 start = ar->lleft + 1;
4612 }
4613 if (ar->pright && start + size - 1 >= ar->lright)
4614 size -= start + size - ar->lright;
4615
4616 /*
4617 * Trim allocation request for filesystems with artificially small
4618 * groups.
4619 */
4620 if (size > EXT4_BLOCKS_PER_GROUP(ac->ac_sb)(EXT4_SB(ac->ac_sb)->s_blocks_per_group))
4621 size = EXT4_BLOCKS_PER_GROUP(ac->ac_sb)(EXT4_SB(ac->ac_sb)->s_blocks_per_group);
4622
4623 end = start + size;
4624
4625 ext4_mb_pa_adjust_overlap(ac, &start, &end);
4626
4627 size = end - start;
4628
4629 /*
4630 * In this function "start" and "size" are normalized for better
4631 * alignment and length such that we could preallocate more blocks.
4632 * This normalization is done such that original request of
4633 * ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical & fe_len should always lie within "start" and
4634 * "size" boundaries.
4635 * (Note fe_len can be relaxed since FS block allocation API does not
4636 * provide gurantee on number of contiguous blocks allocation since that
4637 * depends upon free space left, etc).
4638 * In case of inode pa, later we use the allocated blocks
4639 * [pa_pstart + fe_logical - pa_lstart, fe_len/size] from the preallocated
4640 * range of goal/best blocks [start, size] to put it at the
4641 * ac_o_ex.fe_logical extent of this inode.
4642 * (See ext4_mb_use_inode_pa() for more details)
4643 */
4644 if (start + size <= ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical ||
4645 start > ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical) {

, (unsigned long) start, (unsigned long) size, (unsigned long

4676 &ac->ac_g_ex.fe_group,

, orig_size, start); 0; });
4683}
4684
4685static void ext4_mb_collect_stats(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac)
4686{
4687 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(ac->ac_sb);
4688
4689 if (sbi->s_mb_stats && ac->ac_g_ex.fe_len >= 1) {
4690 atomic_inc(&sbi->s_bal_reqs);
4691 atomic_add(ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len, &sbi->s_bal_allocated);
4692 if (ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len >= ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len)
4693 atomic_inc(&sbi->s_bal_success);
4694
4695 atomic_add(ac->ac_found, &sbi->s_bal_ex_scanned);
4696 for (int i=0; i<EXT4_MB_NUM_CRS; i++) {
4697 atomic_add(ac->ac_cX_found[i], &sbi->s_bal_cX_ex_scanned[i]);
4698 }
4699
4700 atomic_add(ac->ac_groups_scanned, &sbi->s_bal_groups_scanned);
4701 if (ac->ac_g_ex.fe_start == ac->ac_b_ex.fe_start &&
4702 ac->ac_g_ex.fe_group == ac->ac_b_ex.fe_group)
4703 atomic_inc(&sbi->s_bal_goals);
4704 /* did we allocate as much as normalizer originally wanted? */
4705 if (ac->ac_f_ex.fe_len == ac->ac_orig_goal_len)
4706 atomic_inc(&sbi->s_bal_len_goals);
4707
4708 if (ac->ac_found > sbi->s_mb_max_to_scan)
4709 atomic_inc(&sbi->s_bal_breaks);
4710 }
4711
4712 if (ac->ac_op == EXT4_MB_HISTORY_ALLOC1)
4713 trace_ext4_mballoc_alloc(ac);
4714 else
4715 trace_ext4_mballoc_prealloc(ac);
4716}
4717
4718/*
4719 * Called on failure; free up any blocks from the inode PA for this
4720 * context. We don't need this for MB_GROUP_PA because we only change
4721 * pa_free in ext4_mb_release_context(), but on failure, we've already
4722 * zeroed out ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len, so group_pa->pa_free is not changed.
4723 */
4724static void ext4_discard_allocated_blocks(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac)
4725{
4726 struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa = ac->ac_pa;
4727 struct ext4_buddy e4b;
4728 int err;
4729
4730 if (pa == NULL((void *)0)) {
4731 if (ac->ac_f_ex.fe_len == 0)
4732 return;
4733 err = ext4_mb_load_buddy(ac->ac_sb, ac->ac_f_ex.fe_group, &e4b);
4734 if (WARN_RATELIMIT(err,({ static struct ratelimit_state _rs = { .lock = (raw_spinlock_t
) { .raw_lock = { { .val = { (0) } } }, }, .interval = (5 * 1000
), .burst = 10, .flags = 0, }; int rtn = !!(err); if (__builtin_expect
(!!(rtn && ___ratelimit(&_rs, __func__)), 0)) ({ int
__ret_warn_on = !!(rtn); if (__builtin_expect(!!(__ret_warn_on
), 0)) do { int __flags = (((9) << 8)) | (1 << 0)
| (1 << 4) ; ({ static void * __attribute__((__used__)
) __attribute__((__section__(".discard.addressable"))) __UNIQUE_ID_addressable___SCK__WARN_trap_1702
= (void *)(uintptr_t)&__SCK__WARN_trap;; (&__SCT__WARN_trap
); })(({ struct bug_entry *bug; asm __inline volatile("lea (2f)(%%rip), %[addr]\n1:\n"
".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"

), [size] "i" (sizeof(struct bug_entry))); bug; }), err); asm
(""); } while (0); __builtin_expect(!!(__ret_warn_on), 0); }
); rtn; })

) { .raw_lock = { { .val = { (0) } } }, }, .interval = (5 * 1000
), .burst = 10, .flags = 0, }; int rtn = !!(err); if (__builtin_expect

__ret_warn_on = !!(rtn); if (__builtin_expect(!!(__ret_warn_on

| (1 << 4) ; ({ static void * __attribute__((__used__)

= (void *)(uintptr_t)&__SCK__WARN_trap;; (&__SCT__WARN_trap

".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"

4736 /*
4737 * This should never happen since we pin the
4738 * folios in the ext4_allocation_context so
4739 * ext4_mb_load_buddy() should never fail.
4740 */
4741 return;
4742 ext4_lock_group(ac->ac_sb, ac->ac_f_ex.fe_group);
4743 mb_free_blocks(ac->ac_inode, &e4b, ac->ac_f_ex.fe_start,
4744 ac->ac_f_ex.fe_len);
4745 ext4_unlock_group(ac->ac_sb, ac->ac_f_ex.fe_group);
4746 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(&e4b);
4747 return;
4748 }
4749 if (pa->pa_type == MB_INODE_PA) {
4750 spin_lock(&pa->pa_lock);
4751 pa->pa_free += ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len;
4752 spin_unlock(&pa->pa_lock);
4753 }
4754}
4755
4756/*
4757 * use blocks preallocated to inode
4758 */

4787/*
4788 * use blocks preallocated to locality group
4789 */
4790static void ext4_mb_use_group_pa(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
4791 struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa)
4792{
4793 unsigned int len = ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len;
4794
4795 ext4_get_group_no_and_offset(ac->ac_sb, pa->pa_pstart,
4796 &ac->ac_b_ex.fe_group,
4797 &ac->ac_b_ex.fe_start);
4798 ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len = len;
4799 ac->ac_status = AC_STATUS_FOUND2;
4800 ac->ac_pa = pa;
4801
4802 /* we don't correct pa_pstart or pa_len here to avoid
4803 * possible race when the group is being loaded concurrently
4804 * instead we correct pa later, after blocks are marked
4805 * in on-disk bitmap -- see ext4_mb_release_context()
4806 * Other CPUs are prevented from allocating from this pa by lg_mutex
4807 */
4808 mb_debug(ac->ac_sb, "use %u/%u from group pa %p\n",({ if (0) _printk("use %u/%u from group pa %p\n", pa->pa_lstart
, len, pa); 0; })
4809 pa->pa_lstart, len, pa)({ if (0) _printk("use %u/%u from group pa %p\n", pa->pa_lstart
, len, pa); 0; });
4810}

4840
4841/*
4842 * check if found pa meets EXT4_MB_HINT_GOAL_ONLY
4843 */
4844static bool
4845ext4_mb_pa_goal_check(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
4846 struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa)
4847{
4848 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(ac->ac_sb);
4849 ext4_fsblk_t start;
4850
4851 if (likely(!(ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_GOAL_ONLY))__builtin_expect(!!(!(ac->ac_flags & 0x0100)), 1))
4852 return true;
4853
4854 /*
4855 * If EXT4_MB_HINT_GOAL_ONLY is set, ac_g_ex will not be adjusted
4856 * in ext4_mb_normalize_request and will keep same with ac_o_ex
4857 * from ext4_mb_initialize_context. Choose ac_g_ex here to keep
4858 * consistent with ext4_mb_find_by_goal.
4859 */
4860 start = pa->pa_pstart +
4861 (ac->ac_g_ex.fe_logical - pa->pa_lstart);
4862 if (ext4_grp_offs_to_block(ac->ac_sb, &ac->ac_g_ex) != start)
4863 return false;
4864
4865 if (ac->ac_g_ex.fe_len > pa->pa_len -
4866 EXT4_B2C(sbi, ac->ac_g_ex.fe_logical - pa->pa_lstart)((ac->ac_g_ex.fe_logical - pa->pa_lstart) >> (sbi
)->s_cluster_bits))
4867 return false;
4868
4869 return true;
4870}

4973 /*
4974 * Step 4: We now have the non deleted left adjacent pa. Only this
4975 * pa can possibly satisfy the request hence check if it overlaps
4976 * original logical start and stop searching if it doesn't.
4977 */
4978 if (ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical >= pa_logical_end(sbi, tmp_pa)) {
4979 spin_unlock(&tmp_pa->pa_lock);
4980 goto try_group_pa;
4981 }

4989 * satisfy this.

4991 spin_unlock(&tmp_pa->pa_lock);

5002 /*
5003 * We found a valid overlapping pa but couldn't use it because
5004 * it had no free blocks. This should ideally never happen
5005 * because:
5006 *
5007 * 1. When a new inode pa is added to rbtree it must have
5008 * pa_free > 0 since otherwise we won't actually need
5009 * preallocation.
5010 *
5011 * 2. An inode pa that is in the rbtree can only have it's
5012 * pa_free become zero when another thread calls:
5013 * ext4_mb_new_blocks
5014 * ext4_mb_use_preallocated
5015 * ext4_mb_use_inode_pa
5016 *
5017 * 3. Further, after the above calls make pa_free == 0, we will
5018 * immediately remove it from the rbtree in:
5019 * ext4_mb_new_blocks
5020 * ext4_mb_release_context
5021 * ext4_mb_put_pa
5022 *
5023 * 4. Since the pa_free becoming 0 and pa_free getting removed
5024 * from tree both happen in ext4_mb_new_blocks, which is always
5025 * called with i_data_sem held for data allocations, we can be
5026 * sure that another process will never see a pa in rbtree with
5027 * pa_free == 0.
5028 */

5057 spin_lock(&tmp_pa->pa_lock);
5058 if (tmp_pa->pa_deleted == 0 &&

5068 if (cpa) {
5069 ext4_mb_use_group_pa(ac, cpa);
5070 return true;
5071 }
5072 return false;
5073}
5074
5075/*
5076 * the function goes through all preallocation in this group and marks them
5077 * used in in-core bitmap. buddy must be generated from this bitmap
5078 * Need to be called with ext4 group lock held
5079 */
5080static noinline_for_stack__attribute__((__noinline__))
5081void ext4_mb_generate_from_pa(struct super_block *sb, void *bitmap,
5082 ext4_group_t group)
5083{
5084 struct ext4_group_info *grp = ext4_get_group_info(sb, group);
5085 struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa;
5086 struct list_head *cur;
5087 ext4_group_t groupnr;
5088 ext4_grpblk_t start;
5089 int preallocated = 0;
5090 int len;
5091
5092 if (!grp)
5093 return;
5094
5095 /* all form of preallocation discards first load group,
5096 * so the only competing code is preallocation use.
5097 * we don't need any locking here
5098 * notice we do NOT ignore preallocations with pa_deleted
5099 * otherwise we could leave used blocks available for
5100 * allocation in buddy when concurrent ext4_mb_put_pa()
5101 * is dropping preallocation
5102 */

(cur, (&grp->bb_prealloc_list)); cur = cur->next) {

pa_group_list)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*(cur)
), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()");
((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(
struct ext4_prealloc_space, pa_group_list))); });
5105 spin_lock(&pa->pa_lock);
5106 ext4_get_group_no_and_offset(sb, pa->pa_pstart,
5107 &groupnr, &start);
5108 len = pa->pa_len;
5109 spin_unlock(&pa->pa_lock);
5110 if (unlikely(len == 0)__builtin_expect(!!(len == 0), 0))
5111 continue;
5112 BUG_ON(groupnr != group)do { if (__builtin_expect(!!(groupnr != group), 0)) do { ({ asm
volatile("1718" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1718b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1718)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" "" : : [fmt] "i"
(""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line] "i" (5112), [
fl] "i" (0), [size] "i" (sizeof(struct bug_entry))); } while (
0); __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0); } while (0);
5113 mb_set_bits(bitmap, start, len);
5114 preallocated += len;
5115 }
5116 mb_debug(sb, "preallocated %d for group %u\n", preallocated, group)({ if (0) _printk("preallocated %d for group %u\n", preallocated
, group); 0; });
5117}
5118
5119static void ext4_mb_mark_pa_deleted(struct super_block *sb,
5120 struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa)
5121{
5122 struct ext4_inode_info *ei;
5123
5124 if (pa->pa_deleted) {
5125 ext4_warning(sb, "deleted pa, type:%d, pblk:%llu, lblk:%u, len:%d\n",__ext4_warning(sb, __func__, 5127, "deleted pa, type:%d, pblk:%llu, lblk:%u, len:%d\n"
, pa->pa_type, pa->pa_pstart, pa->pa_lstart, pa->
pa_len)
5126 pa->pa_type, pa->pa_pstart, pa->pa_lstart,__ext4_warning(sb, __func__, 5127, "deleted pa, type:%d, pblk:%llu, lblk:%u, len:%d\n"
, pa->pa_type, pa->pa_pstart, pa->pa_lstart, pa->
pa_len)
5127 pa->pa_len)__ext4_warning(sb, __func__, 5127, "deleted pa, type:%d, pblk:%llu, lblk:%u, len:%d\n"
, pa->pa_type, pa->pa_pstart, pa->pa_lstart, pa->
pa_len);
5128 return;
5129 }
5130
5131 pa->pa_deleted = 1;
5132
5133 if (pa->pa_type == MB_INODE_PA) {
5134 ei = EXT4_I(pa->pa_inode)(({ void *__mptr = (void *)(pa->pa_inode); _Static_assert(
__builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*(pa->pa_inode)), typeof
(((struct ext4_inode_info *)0)->vfs_inode)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(pa->pa_inode)), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"
); ((struct ext4_inode_info *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(struct
ext4_inode_info, vfs_inode))); }));
5135 atomic_dec(&ei->i_prealloc_active);
5136 }
5137}

5147static void ext4_mb_pa_callback(struct rcu_headcallback_head *head)
5148{
5149 struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa;
5150
5151 pa = container_of(head, struct ext4_prealloc_space, u.pa_rcu)({ void *__mptr = (void *)(head); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(head)), typeof(((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)0)->
u.pa_rcu)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*(head)), typeof
(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"); ((struct
ext4_prealloc_space *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(struct ext4_prealloc_space
, u.pa_rcu))); });
5152 ext4_mb_pa_free(pa);
5153}
5154
5155/*
5156 * drops a reference to preallocated space descriptor
5157 * if this was the last reference and the space is consumed
5158 */
5159static void ext4_mb_put_pa(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
5160 struct super_block *sb, struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa)
5161{
5162 ext4_group_t grp;
5163 ext4_fsblk_t grp_blk;
5164 struct ext4_inode_info *ei = EXT4_I(ac->ac_inode)(({ void *__mptr = (void *)(ac->ac_inode); _Static_assert(
__builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*(ac->ac_inode)), typeof
(((struct ext4_inode_info *)0)->vfs_inode)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(ac->ac_inode)), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"
); ((struct ext4_inode_info *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(struct
ext4_inode_info, vfs_inode))); }));
5165
5166 /* in this short window concurrent discard can set pa_deleted */
5167 spin_lock(&pa->pa_lock);
5168 if (!atomic_dec_and_test(&pa->pa_count) || pa->pa_free != 0) {
5169 spin_unlock(&pa->pa_lock);
5170 return;
5171 }
5172
5173 if (pa->pa_deleted == 1) {
5174 spin_unlock(&pa->pa_lock);
5175 return;
5176 }
5177
5178 ext4_mb_mark_pa_deleted(sb, pa);
5179 spin_unlock(&pa->pa_lock);
5180
5181 grp_blk = pa->pa_pstart;
5182 /*
5183 * If doing group-based preallocation, pa_pstart may be in the
5184 * next group when pa is used up
5185 */
5186 if (pa->pa_type == MB_GROUP_PA)
5187 grp_blk--;
5188
5189 grp = ext4_get_group_number(sb, grp_blk);
5190
5191 /*
5192 * possible race:
5193 *
5194 * P1 (buddy init) P2 (regular allocation)
5195 * find block B in PA
5196 * copy on-disk bitmap to buddy
5197 * mark B in on-disk bitmap
5198 * drop PA from group
5199 * mark all PAs in buddy
5200 *
5201 * thus, P1 initializes buddy with B available. to prevent this
5202 * we make "copy" and "mark all PAs" atomic and serialize "drop PA"
5203 * against that pair
5204 */
5205 ext4_lock_group(sb, grp);
5206 list_del(&pa->pa_group_list);
5207 ext4_unlock_group(sb, grp);
5208
5209 if (pa->pa_type == MB_INODE_PA) {
5210 write_lock(pa->pa_node_lock.inode_lock)_raw_write_lock(pa->pa_node_lock.inode_lock);
5211 rb_erase(&pa->pa_node.inode_node, &ei->i_prealloc_node);
5212 write_unlock(pa->pa_node_lock.inode_lock)_raw_write_unlock(pa->pa_node_lock.inode_lock);
5213 ext4_mb_pa_free(pa);
5214 } else {
5215 spin_lock(pa->pa_node_lock.lg_lock);
5216 list_del_rcu(&pa->pa_node.lg_list);
5217 spin_unlock(pa->pa_node_lock.lg_lock);
5218 call_rcu(&(pa)->u.pa_rcu, ext4_mb_pa_callback);
5219 }
5220}
5221
5222static void ext4_mb_pa_rb_insert(struct rb_root *root, struct rb_node *new)
5223{
5224 struct rb_node **iter = &root->rb_node, *parent = NULL((void *)0);
5225 struct ext4_prealloc_space *iter_pa, *new_pa;
5226 ext4_lblk_t iter_start, new_start;
5227
5228 while (*iter) {
5229 iter_pa = rb_entry(*iter, struct ext4_prealloc_space,({ void *__mptr = (void *)(*iter); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(*iter)), typeof(((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)0)->
pa_node.inode_node)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*
(*iter)), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"
); ((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof
(struct ext4_prealloc_space, pa_node.inode_node))); })
5230 pa_node.inode_node)({ void *__mptr = (void *)(*iter); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(*iter)), typeof(((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)0)->
pa_node.inode_node)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*
(*iter)), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"
); ((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof
(struct ext4_prealloc_space, pa_node.inode_node))); });
5231 new_pa = rb_entry(new, struct ext4_prealloc_space,({ void *__mptr = (void *)(new); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(new)), typeof(((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)0)->
pa_node.inode_node)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*
(new)), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"
); ((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof
(struct ext4_prealloc_space, pa_node.inode_node))); })
5232 pa_node.inode_node)({ void *__mptr = (void *)(new); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(new)), typeof(((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)0)->
pa_node.inode_node)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*
(new)), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"
); ((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof
(struct ext4_prealloc_space, pa_node.inode_node))); });
5233 iter_start = iter_pa->pa_lstart;
5234 new_start = new_pa->pa_lstart;
5235
5236 parent = *iter;
5237 if (new_start < iter_start)
5238 iter = &((*iter)->rb_left);
5239 else
5240 iter = &((*iter)->rb_right);
5241 }
5242
5243 rb_link_node(new, parent, iter);
5244 rb_insert_color(new, root);
5245}

(""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line] "i" (5284), [

0); __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0); } while (0);
5285
5286 /*
5287 * Use the below logic for adjusting best extent as it keeps
5288 * fragmentation in check while ensuring logical range of best
5289 * extent doesn't overflow out of goal extent:
5290 *
5291 * 1. Check if best ex can be kept at end of goal (before
5292 * cr_best_avail trimmed it) and still cover original start
5293 * 2. Else, check if best ex can be kept at start of goal and
5294 * still cover original end
5295 * 3. Else, keep the best ex at start of original request.
5296 */
5297 ex.fe_len = ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len;
5298
5299 ex.fe_logical = orig_goal_end - EXT4_C2B(sbi, ex.fe_len)((ex.fe_len) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits);
5300 if (ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical >= ex.fe_logical)
5301 goto adjust_bex;
5302
5303 ex.fe_logical = ac->ac_g_ex.fe_logical;
5304 if (o_ex_end <= extent_logical_end(sbi, &ex))
5305 goto adjust_bex;
5306
5307 ex.fe_logical = ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical;
5308adjust_bex:
5309 ac->ac_b_ex.fe_logical = ex.fe_logical;

5311 BUG_ON(ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical < ac->ac_b_ex.fe_logical)do { if (__builtin_expect(!!(ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical < ac
