5399
5400static void ext4_mb_new_preallocation(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac)
5401{
5402 if (ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_GROUP_ALLOC0x0080)
5403 ext4_mb_new_group_pa(ac);
5404 else
5405 ext4_mb_new_inode_pa(ac);
5406}
5407
5408/*
5409 * finds all unused blocks in on-disk bitmap, frees them in
5410 * in-core bitmap and buddy.
5411 * @pa must be unlinked from inode and group lists, so that
5412 * nobody else can find/use it.
5413 * the caller MUST hold group/inode locks.
5414 * TODO: optimize the case when there are no in-core structures yet
5415 */
5416static noinline_for_stack__attribute__((__noinline__)) void
5417ext4_mb_release_inode_pa(struct ext4_buddy *e4b, struct buffer_head *bitmap_bh,
5418 struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa)
5419{
5420 struct super_block *sb = e4b->bd_sb;
5421 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(sb);
5422 unsigned int end;
5423 unsigned int next;
5424 ext4_group_t group;
5425 ext4_grpblk_t bit;
5426 unsigned long long grp_blk_start;
5427 int free = 0;
5428
5429 BUG_ON(pa->pa_deleted == 0)do { if (__builtin_expect(!!(pa->pa_deleted == 0), 0)) do {
({ asm volatile("1735" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1735b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1735)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" "" : : [fmt] "i"
(""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line] "i" (5429), [
fl] "i" (0), [size] "i" (sizeof(struct bug_entry))); } while (
0); __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0); } while (0);
5430 ext4_get_group_no_and_offset(sb, pa->pa_pstart, &group, &bit);
5431 grp_blk_start = pa->pa_pstart - EXT4_C2B(sbi, bit)((bit) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits);
5432 BUG_ON(group != e4b->bd_group && pa->pa_len != 0)do { if (__builtin_expect(!!(group != e4b->bd_group &&
pa->pa_len != 0), 0)) do { ({ asm volatile("1736" ": nop\n\t"
".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1736b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1736)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" "" : : [fmt] "i"
(""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line] "i" (5432), [
fl] "i" (0), [size] "i" (sizeof(struct bug_entry))); } while (
0); __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0); } while (0);
5433 end = bit + pa->pa_len;
5434
5435 while (bit < end) {
5436 bit = mb_find_next_zero_bit(bitmap_bh->b_data, end, bit);
5437 if (bit >= end)
5438 break;
5439 next = mb_find_next_bit(bitmap_bh->b_data, end, bit);
5440 mb_debug(sb, "free preallocated %u/%u in group %u\n",({ if (0) _printk("free preallocated %u/%u in group %u\n", (unsigned
) ext4_group_first_block_no(sb, group) + bit, (unsigned) next
- bit, (unsigned) group); 0; })
5441 (unsigned) ext4_group_first_block_no(sb, group) + bit,({ if (0) _printk("free preallocated %u/%u in group %u\n", (unsigned
) ext4_group_first_block_no(sb, group) + bit, (unsigned) next
- bit, (unsigned) group); 0; })
5442 (unsigned) next - bit, (unsigned) group)({ if (0) _printk("free preallocated %u/%u in group %u\n", (unsigned
) ext4_group_first_block_no(sb, group) + bit, (unsigned) next
- bit, (unsigned) group); 0; });
5443 free += next - bit;
5444
5445 trace_ext4_mballoc_discard(sb, NULL((void *)0), group, bit, next - bit);
5446 trace_ext4_mb_release_inode_pa(pa, (grp_blk_start +
5447 EXT4_C2B(sbi, bit)((bit) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits)),
5448 next - bit);
5449 mb_free_blocks(pa->pa_inode, e4b, bit, next - bit);
5450 bit = next + 1;
5451 }
5452 if (free != pa->pa_free) {
5453 ext4_msg(e4b->bd_sb, KERN_CRIT,__ext4_msg(e4b->bd_sb, "\001" "2", "pa %p: logic %lu, phys. %lu, len %d"
, pa, (unsigned long) pa->pa_lstart, (unsigned long) pa->
pa_pstart, pa->pa_len)
5454 "pa %p: logic %lu, phys. %lu, len %d",__ext4_msg(e4b->bd_sb, "\001" "2", "pa %p: logic %lu, phys. %lu, len %d"
, pa, (unsigned long) pa->pa_lstart, (unsigned long) pa->
pa_pstart, pa->pa_len)
5455 pa, (unsigned long) pa->pa_lstart,__ext4_msg(e4b->bd_sb, "\001" "2", "pa %p: logic %lu, phys. %lu, len %d"
, pa, (unsigned long) pa->pa_lstart, (unsigned long) pa->
pa_pstart, pa->pa_len)
5456 (unsigned long) pa->pa_pstart,__ext4_msg(e4b->bd_sb, "\001" "2", "pa %p: logic %lu, phys. %lu, len %d"
, pa, (unsigned long) pa->pa_lstart, (unsigned long) pa->
pa_pstart, pa->pa_len)
5457 pa->pa_len)__ext4_msg(e4b->bd_sb, "\001" "2", "pa %p: logic %lu, phys. %lu, len %d"
, pa, (unsigned long) pa->pa_lstart, (unsigned long) pa->
pa_pstart, pa->pa_len);
5458 ext4_grp_locked_error(sb, group, 0, 0, "free %u, pa_free %u",__ext4_grp_locked_error(__func__, 5459, sb, group, 0, 0, "free %u, pa_free %u"
, free, pa->pa_free)
5459 free, pa->pa_free)__ext4_grp_locked_error(__func__, 5459, sb, group, 0, 0, "free %u, pa_free %u"
, free, pa->pa_free);
5460 /*
5461 * pa is already deleted so we use the value obtained
5462 * from the bitmap and continue.
5463 */
5464 }
5465 atomic_add(free, &sbi->s_mb_discarded);
5466}
5467
5468static noinline_for_stack__attribute__((__noinline__)) void
5469ext4_mb_release_group_pa(struct ext4_buddy *e4b,
5470 struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa)
5471{
5472 struct super_block *sb = e4b->bd_sb;
5473 ext4_group_t group;
5474 ext4_grpblk_t bit;

5489/*

5493 * - when do we discard

5598/*
5599 * releases all non-used preallocated blocks for given inode
5600 *
5601 * It's important to discard preallocations under i_data_sem
5602 * We don't want another block to be served from the prealloc
5603 * space when we are discarding the inode prealloc space.

5605 * FIXME!! Make sure it is valid at all the call sites

5651 }
5652 if (pa->pa_deleted == 0) {
5653 ext4_mb_mark_pa_deleted(sb, pa);
5654 spin_unlock(&pa->pa_lock);
5655 rb_erase(&pa->pa_node.inode_node, &ei->i_prealloc_node);
5656 list_add(&pa->u.pa_tmp_list, &list);
5657 continue;
5658 }
5659
5660 /* someone is deleting pa right now */
5661 spin_unlock(&pa->pa_lock);
5662 write_unlock(&ei->i_prealloc_lock)_raw_write_unlock(&ei->i_prealloc_lock);
5663
5664 /* we have to wait here because pa_deleted
5665 * doesn't mean pa is already unlinked from
5666 * the list. as we might be called from
5667 * ->clear_inode() the inode will get freed
5668 * and concurrent thread which is unlinking
5669 * pa from inode's list may access already
5670 * freed memory, bad-bad-bad */
5671
5672 /* XXX: if this happens too often, we can
5673 * add a flag to force wait only in case
5674 * of ->clear_inode(), but not in case of
5675 * regular truncate */
5676 schedule_timeout_uninterruptible(HZ1000);
5677 goto repeat;
5678 }
5679 write_unlock(&ei->i_prealloc_lock)_raw_write_unlock(&ei->i_prealloc_lock);
5680
5681 list_for_each_entry_safe(pa, tmp, &list, u.pa_tmp_list)for (pa = ({ void *__mptr = (void *)((&list)->next); _Static_assert
(__builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*((&list)->next))

); ((typeof(*pa) *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(typeof(*pa), u

); ((typeof(*(tmp)) *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(typeof(*(tmp
)), u.pa_tmp_list))); })) {
5682 BUG_ON(pa->pa_type != MB_INODE_PA)do { if (__builtin_expect(!!(pa->pa_type != MB_INODE_PA), 0
)) do { ({ asm volatile("1741" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1741b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1741)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" "" : : [fmt] "i"
(""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line] "i" (5682), [
fl] "i" (0), [size] "i" (sizeof(struct bug_entry))); } while (
0); __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0); } while (0);
5683 group = ext4_get_group_number(sb, pa->pa_pstart);
5684
5685 err = ext4_mb_load_buddy_gfp(sb, group, &e4b,
5686 GFP_NOFS((( gfp_t)(((((1UL))) << (___GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM_BIT))|(
(((1UL))) << (___GFP_KSWAPD_RECLAIM_BIT)))) | (( gfp_t)
((((1UL))) << (___GFP_IO_BIT))))|__GFP_NOFAIL(( gfp_t)((((1UL))) << (___GFP_NOFAIL_BIT))));
5687 if (err) {
5688 ext4_error_err(sb, -err, "Error %d loading buddy information for %u",__ext4_error((sb), __func__, 5689, false, (-err), 0, ("Error %d loading buddy information for %u"
), err, group)
5689 err, group)__ext4_error((sb), __func__, 5689, false, (-err), 0, ("Error %d loading buddy information for %u"
), err, group);
5690 continue;
5691 }
5692
5693 bitmap_bh = ext4_read_block_bitmap(sb, group);
5694 if (IS_ERR(bitmap_bh)) {
5695 err = PTR_ERR(bitmap_bh);
5696 ext4_error_err(sb, -err, "Error %d reading block bitmap for %u",__ext4_error((sb), __func__, 5697, false, (-err), 0, ("Error %d reading block bitmap for %u"
), err, group)
5697 err, group)__ext4_error((sb), __func__, 5697, false, (-err), 0, ("Error %d reading block bitmap for %u"
), err, group);
5698 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(&e4b);
5699 continue;
5700 }
5701
5702 ext4_lock_group(sb, group);
5703 list_del(&pa->pa_group_list);
5704 ext4_mb_release_inode_pa(&e4b, bitmap_bh, pa);
5705 ext4_unlock_group(sb, group);
5706
5707 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(&e4b);
5708 put_bh(bitmap_bh);
5709
5710 list_del(&pa->u.pa_tmp_list);
5711 ext4_mb_pa_free(pa);
5712 }
5713}

5736 * current function is only called due to an error or due to

5744#ifdef CONFIG_EXT4_DEBUG
5745static inlineinline __attribute__((__gnu_inline__)) __attribute__((__unused__
)) __attribute__((no_instrument_function)) void ext4_mb_show_pa(struct super_block *sb)
5746{
5747 ext4_group_t i, ngroups;
5748
5749 if (ext4_emergency_state(sb))
5750 return;
5751
5752 ngroups = ext4_get_groups_count(sb);
5753 mb_debug(sb, "groups: ")({ if (0) _printk("groups: "); 0; });
5754 for (i = 0; i < ngroups; i++) {
5755 struct ext4_group_info *grp = ext4_get_group_info(sb, i);
5756 struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa;
5757 ext4_grpblk_t start;
5758 struct list_head *cur;
5759
5760 if (!grp)
5761 continue;
5762 ext4_lock_group(sb, i);
5763 list_for_each(cur, &grp->bb_prealloc_list)for (cur = (&grp->bb_prealloc_list)->next; !list_is_head
(cur, (&grp->bb_prealloc_list)); cur = cur->next) {
5764 pa = list_entry(cur, struct ext4_prealloc_space,({ void *__mptr = (void *)(cur); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(cur)), typeof(((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)0)->
pa_group_list)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*(cur)
), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()");
((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(
struct ext4_prealloc_space, pa_group_list))); })
5765 pa_group_list)({ void *__mptr = (void *)(cur); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(cur)), typeof(((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)0)->
pa_group_list)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*(cur)
), typeof(void)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()");
((struct ext4_prealloc_space *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(
struct ext4_prealloc_space, pa_group_list))); });
5766 spin_lock(&pa->pa_lock);
5767 ext4_get_group_no_and_offset(sb, pa->pa_pstart,
5768 NULL((void *)0), &start);
5769 spin_unlock(&pa->pa_lock);
5770 mb_debug(sb, "PA:%u:%d:%d\n", i, start,({ if (0) _printk("PA:%u:%d:%d\n", i, start, pa->pa_len); 0
; })
5771 pa->pa_len)({ if (0) _printk("PA:%u:%d:%d\n", i, start, pa->pa_len); 0
; });
5772 }
5773 ext4_unlock_group(sb, i);
5774 mb_debug(sb, "%u: %d/%d\n", i, grp->bb_free,({ if (0) _printk("%u: %d/%d\n", i, grp->bb_free, grp->
bb_fragments); 0; })
5775 grp->bb_fragments)({ if (0) _printk("%u: %d/%d\n", i, grp->bb_free, grp->
bb_fragments); 0; });
5776 }
5777}

)) __attribute__((no_instrument_function)) void ext4_mb_show_pa(struct super_block *sb)

5817static inlineinline __attribute__((__gnu_inline__)) __attribute__((__unused__
)) __attribute__((no_instrument_function)) void ext4_mb_show_ac(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac)
5818{

5822
5823/*
5824 * We use locality group preallocation for small size file. The size of the
5825 * file is determined by the current size or the resulting size after
5826 * allocation which ever is larger
5827 *
5828 * One can tune this size via /sys/fs/ext4/<partition>/mb_stream_req
5829 */
5830static void ext4_mb_group_or_file(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac)
5831{
5832 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(ac->ac_sb);
5833 int bsbits = ac->ac_sb->s_blocksize_bits;
5834 loff_t size, isize;
5835 bool inode_pa_eligible, group_pa_eligible;
5836
5837 if (!(ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_DATA0x0020))
5838 return;
5839
5840 if (unlikely(ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_GOAL_ONLY)__builtin_expect(!!(ac->ac_flags & 0x0100), 0))
5841 return;
5842
5843 group_pa_eligible = sbi->s_mb_group_prealloc > 0;
5844 inode_pa_eligible = true;
5845 size = extent_logical_end(sbi, &ac->ac_o_ex);
5846 isize = (i_size_read(ac->ac_inode) + ac->ac_sb->s_blocksize - 1)
5847 >> bsbits;
5848
5849 /* No point in using inode preallocation for closed files */
5850 if ((size == isize) && !ext4_fs_is_busy(sbi) &&
5851 !inode_is_open_for_write(ac->ac_inode))
5852 inode_pa_eligible = false;
5853
5854 size = max(size, isize)({ __auto_type __UNIQUE_ID_x__1746 = (size); __auto_type __UNIQUE_ID_y__1747
= (isize); do { __attribute__((__noreturn__)) extern void __compiletime_assert_1748
(void) __attribute__((__error__("max""(""size"", ""isize"") signedness error"
))); if (!(!(!(((((typeof(__UNIQUE_ID_x__1746))(-1)) < ( typeof
(__UNIQUE_ID_x__1746))1) ? (2 + (__builtin_constant_p((long long
)(__UNIQUE_ID_x__1746) >= 0) && ((long long)(__UNIQUE_ID_x__1746
) >= 0))) : (1 + 2 * (sizeof(__UNIQUE_ID_x__1746) < 4))
) & ((((typeof(__UNIQUE_ID_y__1747))(-1)) < ( typeof(__UNIQUE_ID_y__1747
))1) ? (2 + (__builtin_constant_p((long long)(__UNIQUE_ID_y__1747
) >= 0) && ((long long)(__UNIQUE_ID_y__1747) >=
0))) : (1 + 2 * (sizeof(__UNIQUE_ID_y__1747) < 4))))))) __compiletime_assert_1748
(); } while (0); ((__UNIQUE_ID_x__1746) > (__UNIQUE_ID_y__1747
) ? (__UNIQUE_ID_x__1746) : (__UNIQUE_ID_y__1747)); });
5855 /* Don't use group allocation for large files */
5856 if (size > sbi->s_mb_stream_request)
5857 group_pa_eligible = false;
5858
5859 if (!group_pa_eligible) {
5860 if (inode_pa_eligible)
5861 ac->ac_flags |= EXT4_MB_STREAM_ALLOC0x0800;
5862 else
5863 ac->ac_flags |= EXT4_MB_HINT_NOPREALLOC0x0040;
5864 return;
5865 }
5866
5867 BUG_ON(ac->ac_lg != NULL)do { if (__builtin_expect(!!(ac->ac_lg != ((void *)0)), 0)
) do { ({ asm volatile("1749" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1749b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1749)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" "" : : [fmt] "i"
(""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line] "i" (5867), [
fl] "i" (0), [size] "i" (sizeof(struct bug_entry))); } while (
0); __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0); } while (0);
5868 /*
5869 * locality group prealloc space are per cpu. The reason for having
5870 * per cpu locality group is to reduce the contention between block
5871 * request from multiple CPUs.
5872 */
5873 ac->ac_lg = raw_cpu_ptr(sbi->s_locality_groups)({ do { const void *__vpp_verify = (typeof((sbi->s_locality_groups
) + 0))((void *)0); (void)__vpp_verify; } while (0); ({ unsigned
long tcp_ptr__ = ({ u64 pfo_val__; asm ("mov" "q " "%%""gs"":"
"%" "[var]" ", %[val]" : [val] "=" "r" (pfo_val__) : [var] "m"
((*(typeof(*(&(this_cpu_off))) *)( uintptr_t)(&(this_cpu_off
))))); (typeof(this_cpu_off))(unsigned long) pfo_val__; }); tcp_ptr__
+= ( unsigned long)(sbi->s_locality_groups); (__typeof__(
*(sbi->s_locality_groups)) *)tcp_ptr__; }); });
5874
5875 /* we're going to use group allocation */
5876 ac->ac_flags |= EXT4_MB_HINT_GROUP_ALLOC0x0080;
5877
5878 /* serialize all allocations in the group */
5879 mutex_lock(&ac->ac_lg->lg_mutex);
5880}
5881
5882static noinline_for_stack__attribute__((__noinline__)) void
5883ext4_mb_initialize_context(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac,
5884 struct ext4_allocation_request *ar)
5885{
5886 struct super_block *sb = ar->inode->i_sb;
5887 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(sb);
5888 struct ext4_super_block *es = sbi->s_es;
5889 ext4_group_t group;
5890 unsigned int len;
5891 ext4_fsblk_t goal;
5892 ext4_grpblk_t block;
5893
5894 /* we can't allocate > group size */
5895 len = ar->len;
5896
5897 /* just a dirty hack to filter too big requests */
5898 if (len >= EXT4_CLUSTERS_PER_GROUP(sb)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_clusters_per_group))
5899 len = EXT4_CLUSTERS_PER_GROUP(sb)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_clusters_per_group);
5900
5901 /* start searching from the goal */
5902 goal = ar->goal;
5903 if (goal < le32_to_cpu(es->s_first_data_block)(( __u32)(__le32)(es->s_first_data_block)) ||
5904 goal >= ext4_blocks_count(es))
5905 goal = le32_to_cpu(es->s_first_data_block)(( __u32)(__le32)(es->s_first_data_block));
5906 ext4_get_group_no_and_offset(sb, goal, &group, &block);
5907
5908 /* set up allocation goals */
5909 ac->ac_b_ex.fe_logical = EXT4_LBLK_CMASK(sbi, ar->logical)((ar->logical) & ~((ext4_lblk_t) (sbi)->s_cluster_ratio
- 1));
5910 ac->ac_status = AC_STATUS_CONTINUE1;
5911 ac->ac_sb = sb;
5912 ac->ac_inode = ar->inode;
5913 ac->ac_o_ex.fe_logical = ac->ac_b_ex.fe_logical;
5914 ac->ac_o_ex.fe_group = group;
5915 ac->ac_o_ex.fe_start = block;
5916 ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len = len;
5917 ac->ac_g_ex = ac->ac_o_ex;
5918 ac->ac_orig_goal_len = ac->ac_g_ex.fe_len;
5919 ac->ac_flags = ar->flags;
5920
5921 /* we have to define context: we'll work with a file or
5922 * locality group. this is a policy, actually */
5923 ext4_mb_group_or_file(ac);
5924
5925 mb_debug(sb, "init ac: %u blocks @ %u, goal %u, flags 0x%x, 2^%d, "({ if (0) _printk("init ac: %u blocks @ %u, goal %u, flags 0x%x, 2^%d, "
"left: %u/%u, right %u/%u to %swritable\n", (unsigned) ar->
len, (unsigned) ar->logical, (unsigned) ar->goal, ac->
ac_flags, ac->ac_2order, (unsigned) ar->lleft, (unsigned
) ar->pleft, (unsigned) ar->lright, (unsigned) ar->pright
, inode_is_open_for_write(ar->inode) ? "" : "non-"); 0; })
5926 "left: %u/%u, right %u/%u to %swritable\n",({ if (0) _printk("init ac: %u blocks @ %u, goal %u, flags 0x%x, 2^%d, "

5957 spin_unlock(&pa->pa_lock);

5966

5970
5971 list_del_rcu(&pa->pa_node.lg_list);
5972 list_add(&pa->u.pa_tmp_list, &discard_list);
5973
5974 total_entries--;
5975 if (total_entries <= 5) {
5976 /*
5977 * we want to keep only 5 entries
5978 * allowing it to grow to 8. This
5979 * mak sure we don't call discard
5980 * soon for this list.
5981 */
5982 break;
5983 }
5984 }
5985 spin_unlock(&lg->lg_prealloc_lock);
5986

5988 int err;

5990 group = ext4_get_group_number(sb, pa->pa_pstart);
5991 err = ext4_mb_load_buddy_gfp(sb, group, &e4b,
5992 GFP_NOFS((( gfp_t)(((((1UL))) << (___GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM_BIT))|(

), err, group)

), err, group);

5997 }
5998 ext4_lock_group(sb, group);
5999 list_del(&pa->pa_group_list);
6000 ext4_mb_release_group_pa(&e4b, pa);
6001 ext4_unlock_group(sb, group);
6002
6003 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(&e4b);
6004 list_del(&pa->u.pa_tmp_list);
6005 call_rcu(&(pa)->u.pa_rcu, ext4_mb_pa_callback);
6006 }
6007}
6008
6009/*
6010 * We have incremented pa_count. So it cannot be freed at this
6011 * point. Also we hold lg_mutex. So no parallel allocation is
6012 * possible from this lg. That means pa_free cannot be updated.
6013 *
6014 * A parallel ext4_mb_discard_group_preallocations is possible.
6015 * which can cause the lg_prealloc_list to be updated.
6016 */
6017
6018static void ext4_mb_add_n_trim(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac)
6019{
6020 int order, added = 0, lg_prealloc_count = 1;
6021 struct super_block *sb = ac->ac_sb;
6022 struct ext4_locality_group *lg = ac->ac_lg;
6023 struct ext4_prealloc_space *tmp_pa, *pa = ac->ac_pa;
6024
6025 order = fls(pa->pa_free) - 1;
6026 if (order > PREALLOC_TB_SIZE10 - 1)
6027 /* The max size of hash table is PREALLOC_TB_SIZE */
6028 order = PREALLOC_TB_SIZE10 - 1;
6029 /* Add the prealloc space to lg */
6030 spin_lock(&lg->lg_prealloc_lock);
6031 list_for_each_entry_rcu(tmp_pa, &lg->lg_prealloc_list[order],for (({ ; }), tmp_pa = ({ void *__mptr = (void *)(({ do { __attribute__
((__noreturn__)) extern void __compiletime_assert_1753(void) __attribute__
((__error__("Unsupported access size for {READ,WRITE}_ONCE()."
))); if (!((sizeof((&lg->lg_prealloc_list[order])->

); ((typeof(*tmp_pa) *)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(typeof(*tmp_pa
), pa_node.lg_list))); })) {
6034 spin_lock(&tmp_pa->pa_lock);
6035 if (tmp_pa->pa_deleted) {
6036 spin_unlock(&tmp_pa->pa_lock);
6037 continue;
6038 }
6039 if (!added && pa->pa_free < tmp_pa->pa_free) {
6040 /* Add to the tail of the previous entry */
6041 list_add_tail_rcu(&pa->pa_node.lg_list,
6042 &tmp_pa->pa_node.lg_list);
6043 added = 1;
6044 /*
6045 * we want to count the total
6046 * number of entries in the list
6047 */
6048 }
6049 spin_unlock(&tmp_pa->pa_lock);
6050 lg_prealloc_count++;
6051 }
6052 if (!added)
6053 list_add_tail_rcu(&pa->pa_node.lg_list,
6054 &lg->lg_prealloc_list[order]);
6055 spin_unlock(&lg->lg_prealloc_lock);
6056
6057 /* Now trim the list to be not more than 8 elements */
6058 if (lg_prealloc_count > 8)
6059 ext4_mb_discard_lg_preallocations(sb, lg,
6060 order, lg_prealloc_count);
6061}
6062
6063/*
6064 * release all resource we used in allocation
6065 */
6066static void ext4_mb_release_context(struct ext4_allocation_context *ac)
6067{
6068 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(ac->ac_sb);
6069 struct ext4_prealloc_space *pa = ac->ac_pa;
6070 if (pa) {
6071 if (pa->pa_type == MB_GROUP_PA) {
6072 /* see comment in ext4_mb_use_group_pa() */
6073 spin_lock(&pa->pa_lock);
6074 pa->pa_pstart += EXT4_C2B(sbi, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len)((ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits);
6075 pa->pa_lstart += EXT4_C2B(sbi, ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len)((ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits);
6076 pa->pa_free -= ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len;
6077 pa->pa_len -= ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len;
6078 spin_unlock(&pa->pa_lock);
6079
6080 /*
6081 * We want to add the pa to the right bucket.
6082 * Remove it from the list and while adding
6083 * make sure the list to which we are adding
6084 * doesn't grow big.
6085 */
6086 if (likely(pa->pa_free)__builtin_expect(!!(pa->pa_free), 1)) {
6087 spin_lock(pa->pa_node_lock.lg_lock);
6088 list_del_rcu(&pa->pa_node.lg_list);
6089 spin_unlock(pa->pa_node_lock.lg_lock);
6090 ext4_mb_add_n_trim(ac);
6091 }
6092 }
6093
6094 ext4_mb_put_pa(ac, ac->ac_sb, pa);
6095 }
6096 if (ac->ac_bitmap_folio)
6097 folio_put(ac->ac_bitmap_folio);
6098 if (ac->ac_buddy_folio)
6099 folio_put(ac->ac_buddy_folio);
6100 if (ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_HINT_GROUP_ALLOC0x0080)
6101 mutex_unlock(&ac->ac_lg->lg_mutex);
6102 ext4_mb_collect_stats(ac);
6103}
6104
6105static int ext4_mb_discard_preallocations(struct super_block *sb, int needed)
6106{
6107 ext4_group_t i, ngroups = ext4_get_groups_count(sb);
6108 int ret;
6109 int freed = 0, busy = 0;
6110 int retry = 0;
6111
6112 trace_ext4_mb_discard_preallocations(sb, needed);
6113
6114 if (needed == 0)
6115 needed = EXT4_CLUSTERS_PER_GROUP(sb)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_clusters_per_group) + 1;
6116 repeat:
6117 for (i = 0; i < ngroups && needed > 0; i++) {
6118 ret = ext4_mb_discard_group_preallocations(sb, i, &busy);
6119 freed += ret;
6120 needed -= ret;
6121 cond_resched()({ __might_resched("fs/ext4/mballoc.c", 6121, 0); _cond_resched
(); });
6122 }
6123
6124 if (needed > 0 && busy && ++retry < 3) {
6125 busy = 0;
6126 goto repeat;
6127 }
6128
6129 return freed;
6130}
6131
6132static bool ext4_mb_discard_preallocations_should_retry(struct super_block *sb,
6133 struct ext4_allocation_context *ac, u64 *seq)
6134{
6135 int freed;
6136 u64 seq_retry = 0;
6137 bool ret = false;
6138
6139 freed = ext4_mb_discard_preallocations(sb, ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len);
6140 if (freed) {
6141 ret = true;
6142 goto out_dbg;
6143 }
6144 seq_retry = ext4_get_discard_pa_seq_sum();
6145 if (!(ac->ac_flags & EXT4_MB_STRICT_CHECK0x4000) || seq_retry != *seq) {
6146 ac->ac_flags |= EXT4_MB_STRICT_CHECK0x4000;
6147 *seq = seq_retry;
6148 ret = true;
6149 }
6150
6151out_dbg:
6152 mb_debug(sb, "freed %d, retry ? %s\n", freed, str_yes_no(ret))({ if (0) _printk("freed %d, retry ? %s\n", freed, str_yes_no
(ret)); 0; });
6153 return ret;
6154}
6155
6156/*
6157 * Simple allocator for Ext4 fast commit replay path. It searches for blocks
6158 * linearly starting at the goal block and also excludes the blocks which
6159 * are going to be in use after fast commit replay.
6160 */
6161static ext4_fsblk_t
6162ext4_mb_new_blocks_simple(struct ext4_allocation_request *ar, int *errp)
6163{
6164 struct buffer_head *bitmap_bh;
6165 struct super_block *sb = ar->inode->i_sb;
6166 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi = EXT4_SB(sb);
6167 ext4_group_t group, nr;
6168 ext4_grpblk_t blkoff;
6169 ext4_grpblk_t max = EXT4_CLUSTERS_PER_GROUP(sb)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_clusters_per_group);
6170 ext4_grpblk_t i = 0;
6171 ext4_fsblk_t goal, block;
6172 struct ext4_super_block *es = sbi->s_es;
6173
6174 goal = ar->goal;
6175 if (goal < le32_to_cpu(es->s_first_data_block)(( __u32)(__le32)(es->s_first_data_block)) ||
6176 goal >= ext4_blocks_count(es))
6177 goal = le32_to_cpu(es->s_first_data_block)(( __u32)(__le32)(es->s_first_data_block));
6178
6179 ar->len = 0;
6180 ext4_get_group_no_and_offset(sb, goal, &group, &blkoff);
6181 for (nr = ext4_get_groups_count(sb); nr > 0; nr--) {
6182 bitmap_bh = ext4_read_block_bitmap(sb, group);
6183 if (IS_ERR(bitmap_bh)) {
6184 *errp = PTR_ERR(bitmap_bh);
6185 pr_warn("Failed to read block bitmap\n")({ do {} while (0); _printk("\001" "4" "Failed to read block bitmap\n"
); });
6186 return 0;
6187 }
6188
6189 while (1) {
6190 i = mb_find_next_zero_bit(bitmap_bh->b_data, max,
6191 blkoff);
6192 if (i >= max)
6193 break;
6194 if (ext4_fc_replay_check_excluded(sb,
6195 ext4_group_first_block_no(sb, group) +
6196 EXT4_C2B(sbi, i)((i) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits))) {
6197 blkoff = i + 1;
6198 } else
6199 break;
6200 }
6201 brelse(bitmap_bh);
6202 if (i < max)
6203 break;
6204
6205 if (++group >= ext4_get_groups_count(sb))
6206 group = 0;
6207
6208 blkoff = 0;
6209 }
6210
6211 if (i >= max) {
6212 *errp = -ENOSPC28;
6213 return 0;
6214 }
6215
6216 block = ext4_group_first_block_no(sb, group) + EXT4_C2B(sbi, i)((i) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits);
6217 ext4_mb_mark_bb(sb, block, 1, true);
6218 ar->len = 1;
6219
6220 *errp = 0;
6221 return block;
6222}
6223
6224/*
6225 * Main entry point into mballoc to allocate blocks
6226 * it tries to use preallocation first, then falls back
6227 * to usual allocation
6228 */
6229ext4_fsblk_t ext4_mb_new_blocks(handle_t *handle,
6230 struct ext4_allocation_request *ar, int *errp)
6231{
6232 struct ext4_allocation_context *ac = NULL((void *)0);
6233 struct ext4_sb_info *sbi;
6234 struct super_block *sb;
6235 ext4_fsblk_t block = 0;
6236 unsigned int inquota = 0;
6237 unsigned int reserv_clstrs = 0;
6238 int retries = 0;
6239 u64 seq;
6240
6241 might_sleep()do { might_resched(); } while (0);
6242 sb = ar->inode->i_sb;
6243 sbi = EXT4_SB(sb);
6244
6245 trace_ext4_request_blocks(ar);
6246 if (sbi->s_mount_state & EXT4_FC_REPLAY0x0020)
6247 return ext4_mb_new_blocks_simple(ar, errp);
6248
6249 /* Allow to use superuser reservation for quota file */
6250 if (ext4_is_quota_file(ar->inode))
6251 ar->flags |= EXT4_MB_USE_ROOT_BLOCKS0x1000;
6252
6253 if ((ar->flags & EXT4_MB_DELALLOC_RESERVED0x0400) == 0) {
6254 /* Without delayed allocation we need to verify
6255 * there is enough free blocks to do block allocation
6256 * and verify allocation doesn't exceed the quota limits.
6257 */
6258 while (ar->len &&
6259 ext4_claim_free_clusters(sbi, ar->len, ar->flags)) {
6260
6261 /* let others to free the space */
6262 cond_resched()({ __might_resched("fs/ext4/mballoc.c", 6262, 0); _cond_resched
(); });
6263 ar->len = ar->len >> 1;
6264 }
6265 if (!ar->len) {
6266 ext4_mb_show_pa(sb);
6267 *errp = -ENOSPC28;
6268 return 0;
6269 }
6270 reserv_clstrs = ar->len;
6271 if (ar->flags & EXT4_MB_USE_ROOT_BLOCKS0x1000) {
6272 dquot_alloc_block_nofail(ar->inode,
6273 EXT4_C2B(sbi, ar->len)((ar->len) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits));
6274 } else {
6275 while (ar->len &&
6276 dquot_alloc_block(ar->inode,
6277 EXT4_C2B(sbi, ar->len)((ar->len) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits))) {
6278
6279 ar->flags |= EXT4_MB_HINT_NOPREALLOC0x0040;
6280 ar->len--;
6281 }
6282 }
6283 inquota = ar->len;
6284 if (ar->len == 0) {
6285 *errp = -EDQUOT122;
6286 goto out;
6287 }
6288 }
6289
6290 ac = kmem_cache_zalloc(ext4_ac_cachep, GFP_NOFS)({ ; ({ typeof(kmem_cache_alloc_noprof(ext4_ac_cachep, (((( gfp_t
)(((((1UL))) << (___GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM_BIT))|((((1UL)))
<< (___GFP_KSWAPD_RECLAIM_BIT)))) | (( gfp_t)((((1UL))
) << (___GFP_IO_BIT)))))|(( gfp_t)((((1UL))) << (
___GFP_ZERO_BIT))))) _res; if (mem_alloc_profiling_enabled())
{ struct alloc_tag * __attribute__((__unused__)) _old; _old =
((void *)0); _res = kmem_cache_alloc_noprof(ext4_ac_cachep, (
((( gfp_t)(((((1UL))) << (___GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM_BIT))|(
(((1UL))) << (___GFP_KSWAPD_RECLAIM_BIT)))) | (( gfp_t)
((((1UL))) << (___GFP_IO_BIT)))))|(( gfp_t)((((1UL))) <<
(___GFP_ZERO_BIT)))); do {} while (0); } else _res = kmem_cache_alloc_noprof
(ext4_ac_cachep, (((( gfp_t)(((((1UL))) << (___GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM_BIT
))|((((1UL))) << (___GFP_KSWAPD_RECLAIM_BIT)))) | (( gfp_t
)((((1UL))) << (___GFP_IO_BIT)))))|(( gfp_t)((((1UL))) <<
(___GFP_ZERO_BIT)))); _res; }); });
6291 if (!ac) {
6292 ar->len = 0;
6293 *errp = -ENOMEM12;
6294 goto out;
6295 }
6296
6297 ext4_mb_initialize_context(ac, ar);
6298
6299 ac->ac_op = EXT4_MB_HISTORY_PREALLOC2;
6300 seq = this_cpu_read(discard_pa_seq)({ __typeof__(discard_pa_seq) pscr_ret__; do { const void *__vpp_verify
= (typeof((&(discard_pa_seq)) + 0))((void *)0); (void)__vpp_verify
; } while (0); switch(sizeof(discard_pa_seq)) { case 1: pscr_ret__
= ({ u8 pfo_val__; asm volatile ("mov" "b " "%%""gs"":" "%" "[var]"
", %[val]" : [val] "=" "q" (pfo_val__) : [var] "m" ((*(typeof
(*(&(discard_pa_seq))) *)( uintptr_t)(&(discard_pa_seq
))))); (typeof(discard_pa_seq))(unsigned long) pfo_val__; });
break; case 2: pscr_ret__ = ({ u16 pfo_val__; asm volatile (
"mov" "w " "%%""gs"":" "%" "[var]" ", %[val]" : [val] "=" "r"
(pfo_val__) : [var] "m" ((*(typeof(*(&(discard_pa_seq)))
*)( uintptr_t)(&(discard_pa_seq))))); (typeof(discard_pa_seq
))(unsigned long) pfo_val__; }); break; case 4: pscr_ret__ = (
{ u32 pfo_val__; asm volatile ("mov" "l " "%%""gs"":" "%" "[var]"
", %[val]" : [val] "=" "r" (pfo_val__) : [var] "m" ((*(typeof
(*(&(discard_pa_seq))) *)( uintptr_t)(&(discard_pa_seq
))))); (typeof(discard_pa_seq))(unsigned long) pfo_val__; });
break; case 8: pscr_ret__ = ({ u64 pfo_val__; asm volatile (
"mov" "q " "%%""gs"":" "%" "[var]" ", %[val]" : [val] "=" "r"
(pfo_val__) : [var] "m" ((*(typeof(*(&(discard_pa_seq)))
*)( uintptr_t)(&(discard_pa_seq))))); (typeof(discard_pa_seq
))(unsigned long) pfo_val__; }); break; default: __bad_size_call_parameter
(); break; } pscr_ret__; });
6301 if (!ext4_mb_use_preallocated(ac)) {
6302 ac->ac_op = EXT4_MB_HISTORY_ALLOC1;
6303 ext4_mb_normalize_request(ac, ar);
6304
6305 *errp = ext4_mb_pa_alloc(ac);
6306 if (*errp)
6307 goto errout;
6308repeat:
6309 /* allocate space in core */
6310 *errp = ext4_mb_regular_allocator(ac);
6311 /*
6312 * pa allocated above is added to grp->bb_prealloc_list only
6313 * when we were able to allocate some block i.e. when
6314 * ac->ac_status == AC_STATUS_FOUND.
6315 * And error from above mean ac->ac_status != AC_STATUS_FOUND
6316 * So we have to free this pa here itself.
6317 */
6318 if (*errp) {
6319 ext4_mb_pa_put_free(ac);
6320 ext4_discard_allocated_blocks(ac);
6321 goto errout;
6322 }
6323 if (ac->ac_status == AC_STATUS_FOUND2 &&
6324 ac->ac_o_ex.fe_len >= ac->ac_f_ex.fe_len)
6325 ext4_mb_pa_put_free(ac);
6326 }
6327 if (likely(ac->ac_status == AC_STATUS_FOUND)__builtin_expect(!!(ac->ac_status == 2), 1)) {
6328 *errp = ext4_mb_mark_diskspace_used(ac, handle);
6329 if (*errp) {
6330 ext4_discard_allocated_blocks(ac);
6331 goto errout;
6332 } else {
6333 block = ext4_grp_offs_to_block(sb, &ac->ac_b_ex);
6334 ar->len = ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len;
6335 }
6336 } else {
6337 if (++retries < 3 &&
6338 ext4_mb_discard_preallocations_should_retry(sb, ac, &seq))
6339 goto repeat;
6340 /*
6341 * If block allocation fails then the pa allocated above
6342 * needs to be freed here itself.
6343 */
6344 ext4_mb_pa_put_free(ac);
6345 *errp = -ENOSPC28;
6346 }
6347
6348 if (*errp) {
6349errout:
6350 ac->ac_b_ex.fe_len = 0;
6351 ar->len = 0;
6352 ext4_mb_show_ac(ac);
6353 }
6354 ext4_mb_release_context(ac);
6355 kmem_cache_free(ext4_ac_cachep, ac);
6356out:
6357 if (inquota && ar->len < inquota)
6358 dquot_free_block(ar->inode, EXT4_C2B(sbi, inquota - ar->len)((inquota - ar->len) << (sbi)->s_cluster_bits));
6359 /* release any reserved blocks */
6360 if (reserv_clstrs)
6361 percpu_counter_sub(&sbi->s_dirtyclusters_counter, reserv_clstrs);
6362
6363 trace_ext4_allocate_blocks(ar, (unsigned long long)block);
6364
6365 return block;
6366}
6367
6368/*
6369 * We can merge two free data extents only if the physical blocks
6370 * are contiguous, AND the extents were freed by the same transaction,
6371 * AND the blocks are associated with the same group.
6372 */
6373static inlineinline __attribute__((__gnu_inline__)) __attribute__((__unused__
)) __attribute__((no_instrument_function)) bool
6374ext4_freed_extents_can_be_merged(struct ext4_free_data *entry1,
6375 struct ext4_free_data *entry2)
6376{
6377 if (entry1->efd_tid != entry2->efd_tid)
6378 return false;
6379 if (entry1->efd_start_cluster + entry1->efd_count !=
6380 entry2->efd_start_cluster)
6381 return false;
6382 if (WARN_ON_ONCE(entry1->efd_group != entry2->efd_group)({ int __ret_warn_on = !!(entry1->efd_group != entry2->
efd_group); if (__builtin_expect(!!(__ret_warn_on), 0)) do { __auto_type
__flags = (1 << 0)|((1 << 1) | ((9) << 8))
; ({ asm volatile("1755" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1755b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1755)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1b - ., 8; .popsection"
: : [fmt] "i" (""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line]
"i" (6382), [fl] "i" (__flags), [size] "i" (sizeof(struct bug_entry
))); } while (0); ({ asm volatile("1756" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1756b - ., 4; .popsection"
: : "i" (1756)); }); } while (0); __builtin_expect(!!(__ret_warn_on
), 0); }))
6383 return false;
6384 return true;
6385}
6386
6387static inlineinline __attribute__((__gnu_inline__)) __attribute__((__unused__
)) __attribute__((no_instrument_function)) void
6388ext4_merge_freed_extents(struct ext4_sb_info *sbi, struct rb_root *root,
6389 struct ext4_free_data *entry1,
6390 struct ext4_free_data *entry2)
6391{
6392 entry1->efd_count += entry2->efd_count;
6393 spin_lock(&sbi->s_md_lock);
6394 list_del(&entry2->efd_list);
6395 spin_unlock(&sbi->s_md_lock);
6396 rb_erase(&entry2->efd_node, root);
6397 kmem_cache_free(ext4_free_data_cachep, entry2);
6398}
6399
6400static inlineinline __attribute__((__gnu_inline__)) __attribute__((__unused__
)) __attribute__((no_instrument_function)) void
6401ext4_try_merge_freed_extent_prev(struct ext4_sb_info *sbi, struct rb_root *root,
6402 struct ext4_free_data *entry)
6403{
6404 struct ext4_free_data *prev;
6405 struct rb_node *node;
6406
6407 node = rb_prev(&entry->efd_node);
6408 if (!node)
6409 return;
6410
6411 prev = rb_entry(node, struct ext4_free_data, efd_node)({ void *__mptr = (void *)(node); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(node)), typeof(((struct ext4_free_data *)0)->efd_node
)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*(node)), typeof(void
)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"); ((struct ext4_free_data
*)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(struct ext4_free_data, efd_node
))); });
6412 if (ext4_freed_extents_can_be_merged(prev, entry))
6413 ext4_merge_freed_extents(sbi, root, prev, entry);
6414}
6415
6416static inlineinline __attribute__((__gnu_inline__)) __attribute__((__unused__
)) __attribute__((no_instrument_function)) void
6417ext4_try_merge_freed_extent_next(struct ext4_sb_info *sbi, struct rb_root *root,
6418 struct ext4_free_data *entry)
6419{
6420 struct ext4_free_data *next;
6421 struct rb_node *node;
6422
6423 node = rb_next(&entry->efd_node);
6424 if (!node)
6425 return;
6426
6427 next = rb_entry(node, struct ext4_free_data, efd_node)({ void *__mptr = (void *)(node); _Static_assert(__builtin_types_compatible_p
(typeof(*(node)), typeof(((struct ext4_free_data *)0)->efd_node
)) || __builtin_types_compatible_p(typeof(*(node)), typeof(void
)), "pointer type mismatch in container_of()"); ((struct ext4_free_data
*)(__mptr - __builtin_offsetof(struct ext4_free_data, efd_node
))); });
6428 if (ext4_freed_extents_can_be_merged(entry, next))
6429 ext4_merge_freed_extents(sbi, root, entry, next);
6430}

6494 kmem_cache_free(ext4_free_data_cachep, new_entry);

6504 spin_unlock(&sbi->s_md_lock);
6505}
6506
6507static void ext4_free_blocks_simple(struct inode *inode, ext4_fsblk_t block,
6508 unsigned long count)
6509{
6510 struct super_block *sb = inode->i_sb;
6511 ext4_group_t group;
6512 ext4_grpblk_t blkoff;
6513
6514 ext4_get_group_no_and_offset(sb, block, &group, &blkoff);
6515 ext4_mb_mark_context(NULL((void *)0), sb, false, group, blkoff, count,
6516 EXT4_MB_BITMAP_MARKED_CHECK0x0001 |
6517 EXT4_MB_SYNC_UPDATE0x0002,
6518 NULL((void *)0));
6519}
6520
6521/**

6785/**
6786 * ext4_group_add_blocks() -- Add given blocks to an existing group
6787 * @handle: handle to this transaction
6788 * @sb: super block
6789 * @block: start physical block to add to the block group

, block_group);

6821 goto error_out;
6822 }
6823
6824 err = ext4_mb_load_buddy(sb, block_group, &e4b);
6825 if (err)
6826 goto error_out;
6827
6828 if (!ext4_sb_block_valid(sb, NULL((void *)0), block, count)) {

"Block = %llu, count = %lu"), block, count)
6831 block, count)__ext4_error((sb), __func__, 6831, false, 0, 0, ("Adding blocks in system zones - "

6837 cluster_count, EXT4_MB_BITMAP_MARKED_CHECK0x0001,
6838 &changed);

), block_group);
6844
6845 ext4_lock_group(sb, block_group);

6847 ext4_unlock_group(sb, block_group);
6848 percpu_counter_add(&sbi->s_freeclusters_counter,

6858/**
6859 * ext4_trim_extent -- function to TRIM one single free extent in the group
6860 * @sb: super block for the file system
6861 * @start: starting block of the free extent in the alloc. group
6862 * @count: number of blocks to TRIM
6863 * @e4b: ext4 buddy for the group

(ext4_group_lock_ptr(sb, group))->rlock)->raw_lock)), 0
)) do { ({ asm volatile("1763" ": nop\n\t" ".pushsection .discard.annotate_insn, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 1763b - ., 3; .popsection"
: : "i" (1763)); }); do { asm __inline volatile("1:\t" " " "ud2"
" " "\n" ".pushsection __bug_table,\"aw\"\n\t" "912: " ".pushsection .discard.annotate_data, \"M\", @progbits, 8; .long 912b - ., 1; .popsection"
"\n\t" "2:\n\t" "\t.long 1b - ." "\t# bug_entry::bug_addr\n"
"\t.long " "%c[fmt]" " - .\t# bug_entry::format\n" "\t.long "
"%c[file]" " - .\t# bug_entry::file\n" "\t.word " "%c[line]"
"\t# bug_entry::line\n" "\t.word " "%c[fl]" "\t# bug_entry::flags\n"
"\t.org 2b + " "%c[size]" "\n" ".popsection\n" "" : : [fmt] "i"
(""), [file] "i" ("fs/ext4/mballoc.c"), [line] "i" (6880), [

0); __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0); } while (0);
6881
6882 ex.fe_start = start;
6883 ex.fe_group = group;
6884 ex.fe_len = count;
6885
6886 /*
6887 * Mark blocks used, so no one can reuse them while
6888 * being trimmed.
6889 */
6890 mb_mark_used(e4b, &ex);
6891 ext4_unlock_group(sb, group);
6892 ret = ext4_issue_discard(sb, group, start, count);
6893 ext4_lock_group(sb, group);
6894 mb_free_blocks(NULL((void *)0), e4b, start, ex.fe_len);
6895 return ret;
6896}

6898static ext4_grpblk_t ext4_last_grp_cluster(struct super_block *sb,
6899 ext4_group_t grp)
6900{
6901 unsigned long nr_clusters_in_group;
6902
6903 if (grp < (ext4_get_groups_count(sb) - 1))
6904 nr_clusters_in_group = EXT4_CLUSTERS_PER_GROUP(sb)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_clusters_per_group);
6905 else
6906 nr_clusters_in_group = (ext4_blocks_count(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_es) -
6907 ext4_group_first_block_no(sb, grp))
6908 >> EXT4_CLUSTER_BITS(sb)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_cluster_bits);
6909
6910 return nr_clusters_in_group - 1;
6911}

6978/**
6979 * ext4_trim_all_free -- function to trim all free space in alloc. group
6980 * @sb: super block for file system
6981 * @group: group to be trimmed
6982 * @start: first group block to examine
6983 * @max: last group block to examine
6984 * @minblocks: minimum extent block count
6985 *

, ret, group)
7004 ret, group)__ext4_warning(sb, __func__, 7004, "Error %d loading buddy information for %u"
, ret, group);

7010 if (!EXT4_MB_GRP_WAS_TRIMMED(e4b.bd_info)(((__builtin_constant_p(1) && __builtin_constant_p((uintptr_t
)(&((e4b.bd_info)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t)((void *)0
)) && (uintptr_t)(&((e4b.bd_info)->bb_state)) !=
(uintptr_t)((void *)0) && __builtin_constant_p(*(const
unsigned long *)(&((e4b.bd_info)->bb_state)))) ? const_test_bit
(1, &((e4b.bd_info)->bb_state)) : _test_bit(1, &((
e4b.bd_info)->bb_state)))) ||

7012 ret = ext4_try_to_trim_range(sb, &e4b, start, max, minblocks);
7013 else
7014 ret = 0;
7015
7016 ext4_unlock_group(sb, group);
7017 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(&e4b);
7018
7019 ext4_debug("trimmed %d blocks in the group %d\n",({ if (0) _printk("trimmed %d blocks in the group %d\n", ret,
group); 0; })

group); 0; });

7022 return ret;

7025/**
7026 * ext4_trim_fs() -- trim ioctl handle function
7027 * @sb: superblock for filesystem
7028 * @range: fstrim_range structure
7029 *
7030 * start: First Byte to trim
7031 * len: number of Bytes to trim from start
7032 * minlen: minimum extent length in Bytes

(sb))->s_cluster_ratio - 1) >> (EXT4_SB(sb))->s_cluster_bits
)

(sb))->s_cluster_ratio - 1) >> (EXT4_SB(sb))->s_cluster_bits
);

7054 if (minlen > EXT4_CLUSTERS_PER_GROUP(sb)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_clusters_per_group) ||
7055 start >= max_blks ||

7060 minlen = EXT4_NUM_B2C(EXT4_SB(sb),(((discard_granularity >> sb->s_blocksize_bits) + (EXT4_SB
(sb))->s_cluster_ratio - 1) >> (EXT4_SB(sb))->s_cluster_bits
)
7061 discard_granularity >> sb->s_blocksize_bits)(((discard_granularity >> sb->s_blocksize_bits) + (EXT4_SB
(sb))->s_cluster_ratio - 1) >> (EXT4_SB(sb))->s_cluster_bits

7064 }

7066 end = max_blks - 1;

7068 goto out;
7069 if (start < first_data_blk)
7070 start = first_data_blk;
7071
7072 /* Determine first and last group to examine based on start and end */
7073 ext4_get_group_no_and_offset(sb, (ext4_fsblk_t) start,
7074 &first_group, &first_cluster);
7075 ext4_get_group_no_and_offset(sb, (ext4_fsblk_t) end,
7076 &last_group, &last_cluster);
7077
7078 /* end now represents the last cluster to discard in this group */
7079 end = EXT4_CLUSTERS_PER_GROUP(sb)(EXT4_SB(sb)->s_clusters_per_group) - 1;
7080
7081 for (group = first_group; group <= last_group; group++) {
7082 if (ext4_trim_interrupted())
7083 break;
7084 grp = ext4_get_group_info(sb, group);

)0)) && (uintptr_t)(&((grp)->bb_state)) != (uintptr_t

(((1UL))) << (___GFP_KSWAPD_RECLAIM_BIT)))) | (( gfp_t)
((((1UL))) << (___GFP_IO_BIT)))));

7091 break;
7092 }
7093

7095 * For all the groups except the last one, last cluster will
7096 * always be EXT4_CLUSTERS_PER_GROUP(sb)-1, so we only need to
7097 * change it for the last group, note that last_cluster is
7098 * already computed earlier by ext4_get_group_no_and_offset()
7099 */
7100 if (group == last_group)
7101 end = last_cluster;

7103 cnt = ext4_trim_all_free(sb, group, first_cluster,
7104 end, minlen);
7105 if (cnt < 0) {
7106 ret = cnt;

7108 }
7109 trimmed += cnt;

7111

7113 * For every group except the first one, we are sure
7114 * that the first cluster to discard will be cluster #0.
7115 */
7116 first_cluster = 0;
7117 }
7118
7119 if (!ret)
7120 EXT4_SB(sb)->s_last_trim_minblks = minlen;

7123 range->len = EXT4_C2B(EXT4_SB(sb), trimmed)((trimmed) << (EXT4_SB(sb))->s_cluster_bits) << sb->s_blocksize_bits;
7124 return ret;
7125}

7155 start = end;
7156 ext4_unlock_group(sb, group);
7157 error = meta_formatter(sb, group, first, start - first,
7158 priv);
7159 if (error)
7160 goto out_unload;
7161 ext4_lock_group(sb, group);
7162 }
7163 while (start <= end) {
7164 start = mb_find_next_zero_bit(bitmap, end + 1, start);
7165 if (start > end)
7166 break;
7167 next = mb_find_next_bit(bitmap, end + 1, start);
7168
7169 ext4_unlock_group(sb, group);
7170 error = formatter(sb, group, start, next - start, priv);
7171 if (error)
7172 goto out_unload;
7173 ext4_lock_group(sb, group);
7174
7175 start = next + 1;
7176 }
7177
7178 ext4_unlock_group(sb, group);
7179out_unload:
7180 ext4_mb_unload_buddy(&e4b);
7181
7182 return error;
7183}
7184
7185#if IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_EXT4_KUNIT_TESTS)0
7186void mb_clear_bits_test(void *bm, int cur, int len)
7187{
7188 mb_clear_bits(bm, cur, len);
7189}
7190EXPORT_SYMBOL_FOR_EXT4_TEST(mb_clear_bits_test);
